functions.name: BinaryFile.Append
functions.description: Opens a file to append data. See also Text.AppendTextFile . This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The native file path. Something like "/Users/cs/desktop/test.txt" on Mac and "C:\Programs\Data\test.txt" on Windows. Files without path end in the root directory on Mac.

examples.label: Append text to a file:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$fh
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.Append"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.WriteText
";
$fh
; "Hello World") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$fh
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Append"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fh</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.WriteText"; $fh; "Hello World") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $fh) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: BinaryFile.Close
functions.description: Closes a file. Releases memory used for the file.

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

examples.label: Split files into smaller chunks:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$InputPath
; Value: "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" ]
Set Variable [
$ChunkSize
; Value: 10*1024*1024 ]
#
Set Variable [
$InputStream
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Open
";
$inputPath
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to open file." ;
$InputStream
& ¶ &
$InputPath
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$InputLength
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Length
";
$InputStream
) ]
Set Variable [
$InputPosition
; Value: 0 ]
Set Variable [
$Counter
; Value: 1 ]
Loop
# Read chunk
Set Variable [
$Chunk
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.ReadHex
";
$InputStream
;
$ChunkSize
) ]
#
# Write Chunk
Set Variable [
$OutputPath
; Value:
$InputPath
& "." &
$Counter
]
Set Variable [
$OutputStream
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.Create";
$OutputPath
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to create file." ;
$InputStream
& ¶ &
$OutputPath
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$InputStream
) ]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.WriteHex
";
$OutputStream
;
$Chunk
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$OutputStream
) ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$InputPosition
; Value:
$InputPosition
+
$ChunkSize
]
Exit Loop If [
$InputPosition
>
$InputLength
]
Set Variable [
$Counter
; Value:
$Counter
+1 ]
End Loop
#
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$InputStream
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Users/cs/Desktop/test.mp4" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>10*1024*1024 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ChunkSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Open"; $inputPath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputStream</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to open file." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$InputStream & ¶ & $InputPath </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Length"; $InputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputLength</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputPosition</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Counter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Read chunk</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.ReadHex"; $InputStream; $ChunkSize ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Chunk</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write Chunk</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$InputPath & "." & $Counter </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OutputPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Create"; $OutputPath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OutputStream</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to create file." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$InputStream & ¶ & $OutputPath </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $InputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.WriteHex"; $OutputStream; $Chunk ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $OutputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$InputPosition + $ChunkSize </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputPosition</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $InputPosition > $InputLength </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$Counter +1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Counter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $InputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: BinaryFile.EOF
functions.description: Queries whether we are at the end of the current file. Returns 1 if we are at the end or 0 if not.

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

examples.label: Try EOF:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$fh
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Open
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt" ) ]
# Check End Of File usage
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.EOF";
$fh
) ]
# Read a lot of data
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.ReadText
";
$fh
; 10000000; "Latin1") ]
# Check End Of File usage
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.EOF";
$fh
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$fh
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Open"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fh</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Check End Of File usage</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.EOF"; $fh) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Read a lot of data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.ReadText"; $fh; 10000000; "Latin1") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Check End Of File usage</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.EOF"; $fh) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $fh) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: BinaryFile.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of binary file objects. Returns IDs for all BinaryFile objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call BinaryFile.Release in your scripts.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.List" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputLength</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputPosition</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Counter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Read chunk</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.ReadHex"; $InputStream; $ChunkSize ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Chunk</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write Chunk</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$InputPath & "." & $Counter </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OutputPath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Create"; $OutputPath ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$OutputStream</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to create file." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$InputStream & ¶ & $OutputPath </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $InputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result:    </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.WriteHex"; $OutputStream; $Chunk ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $OutputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$InputPosition + $ChunkSize </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InputPosition</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $InputPosition > $InputLength </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$Counter +1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Counter</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; $InputStream ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: BinaryFile.Position
functions.description: Queries current file position.

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

examples.label: Play with position:

$fh
; Value:

BinaryFile.Open

( "
BinaryFile.Length
";
$fh
) ]
# move current position
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

BinaryFile.Seek
";
$fh
; 6) ]

$pos
; Value:

( "BinaryFile.Position";
$fh
) ]

BinaryFile.ReadText
";

; 5; "UTF8") ]
Set Variable [

BinaryFile.Close
";
$fh
) ]

functions.name: BinaryFile.ReadByte

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef

functions.name: BinaryFile.ReadContainer

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef

parameters.name: ByteCount

parameters.name: FileName

functions.name: BinaryFile.ReadInt

functions.name: BinaryFile.ReadPDF
functions.description: Reads data as PDF. This is a special method which actually reads the data and then looks inside the data for a PDF. So there can be data before and after the PDF, which is ignored. Returns nothing if no PDF is found. For PDF content: If you have a DynaPDF Pro license and DynaPDF functions initialized, this function can add previews for the PDF file on Windows and Linux. For MacOS, we make them with PDFKit.

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

parameters.name: ByteCount
parameters.description: The number of Bytes to read.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: Optional file name to use for PDF in container value.

examples.label: Read some PDF from file:

$fh
; Value:

BinaryFile.Open
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/Tes0059.pdf" ) ]
Set Variable [

( "
BinaryFile.Length
";
$fh
) ]

; Value:

$fh
;
$len
; "test.pdf") ]
Set Field [
test::ContainerField
;
$pdf
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$fh
) ]

functions.name: BinaryFile.ReadText
functions.description: Reads text from file. You have to decide which encoding to use (if not UTF8). We support a few encodings, but we can add more if you need something special. Some characters will be wrong if the encoding is not correct. The read text may be shorter than requested if you hit end of file. When passing UTF-16 as encoding, we can detect byte order if there is a Byte Order Mark. See also Text.ReadTextFile and Text.ReadTextFromContainer .

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

parameters.name: ByteCount
parameters.description: The number of Bytes to read.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: PNG file check:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
	path = "/Users/cs/Pictures/test.png";
	stream = MBS( "BinaryFile.Open"; path );
	text = MBS( "BinaryFile.ReadText"; stream; 10; "Windows" );
	r = MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; stream;);
	IsPNG = (Position ( text; "PNG"; 1; 1 ) = 2)
]; IsPNG )

// returns 1 if file is most likely a PNG as it starts with right bytes.
// for a CF, path may be a parameter.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
// returns 1 if file is most likely a PDF as it starts with right bytes.
// for a CF, path may be a parameter.

path = "/Users/cs/Pictures/test.png";
stream = MBS( "BinaryFile.Open"; path );
text = MBS( "BinaryFile.ReadText"; stream; 4; "Windows" );
r = MBS( "BinaryFile.Close"; stream;);
IsPDF = (text == "%PDF")
]; IsPDF )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: BinaryFile.Seek
functions.description: Moves file pointer to new position.

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

parameters.name: Position
parameters.description: The new position in the file.

examples.label: Play with Seek:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$fh
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Open
"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/test.txt" ) ]
# Query length
Set Variable [
$len
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Length
";
$fh
) ]
# move current position
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.Seek";
$fh
; 6) ]
# query current position
Set Variable [
$pos
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Position
";
$fh
) ]
# read 5 bytes
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.ReadText
";
$fh
; 5; "UTF8") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
BinaryFile.Close
";
$fh
) ]

functions.name: BinaryFile.WriteText
functions.description: Writes a text file with the given text. You have to decide which encoding to use (if not UTF-8). We support a few encodings, but we can add more if you need something special. Please use Text.ReplaceNewLine to define which line endings the file has. If text encoding doesn't support given characters, they are converted to close match if possible or replaced by question marks. You can pass UTF-16, UTF-16LE or UTF-16BE for encoding, too. See also Text.WriteTextFile and Text.WriteToContainer .

parameters.name: BinaryFileRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the open file.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to write.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Write Hello:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "BinaryFile.WriteText";
$fh
; "Hello"; "UTF8")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "BinaryFile.WriteText"; $fh; "Hello"; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DialogModifications.Install
functions.description: Installs feature which can rename dialog elements in FileMaker. Those functions are to rename text in a FileMaker dialogs. So before you run an operation which shows one of the built in dialogs, you can install, clear and set the items to replace. After the dialog finished you should call DialogModifications.Clear function. This makes sure you don't replace text when you don't expect this. See also DialogModifications.Uninstall . Added iOS support for MBS Plugin in version 12.2.

examples.label: Install with setup:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DialogModifications.Install" )
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Clear
" )
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Set
"; "Import", "Import of Contacts" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Install" )
MBS( "DialogModifications.Clear" )
MBS( "DialogModifications.Set"; "Import", "Import of Contacts" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Change stop script dialog on iOS:
examples.text_code:
# Install it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DialogModifications.Install" ) ]
# Reset all replacements
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Clear
" ) ]
# Set our replacements
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Set
"; "Yes"; "Continue" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Set
"; "No"; "Stop" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Set
"; "Stop execution of the current script?"; "Do you like to stop the export?" ) ]
# do some important work
Set Variable [
$endTime
; Value: Get(CurrentTimestamp)+10 ]
Loop
Set Field [
Sharing::counter
;
$index
]
# next
Exit Loop If [ Get(CurrentTimestamp)>
$endTime
]
End Loop
# stop for next script
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
DialogModifications.Clear
" ) ]

    <Text> Install it</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Clear" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Set our replacements</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Set"; "Yes"; "Continue" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Set"; "No"; "Stop" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Set"; "Stop execution of the current script?"; "Do you like to stop the export?" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do some important work</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(CurrentTimestamp)+10 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$endTime</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$index </Calculation>
    <Field table="Sharing" id="99999" name="counter "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Get(CurrentTimestamp)> $endTime </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> stop for next script</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DialogModifications.Clear" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DialogModifications.Set
functions.description: Adds an entry to the replacements list. See also DialogModifications.Clear . Be aware that match is exactly byte per byte, so different variants of quotes like " and " or " can make a big difference. Also you may need to provide several variants of text to cover all variants in different FileMaker localizations or versions. Added iOS support for MBS Plugin in version 12.2.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The item to look for. Text must match exactly.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new text to use.

examples.label: Replaces a text:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("DialogModifications.Set"; "Wollen Sie den GESAMTEN Datensatz löschen?"; "Wollen Sie den Kontakt löschen?")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DialogModifications.Set"; "Wollen Sie den GESAMTEN Datensatz löschen?"; "Wollen Sie den Kontakt löschen?")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DialogModifications.Uninstall
functions.description: Uninstalls the feature. You should call this when you no longer need the replacements. See also DialogModifications.Install .

functions.name: DragDrop.AttachToOverlay
functions.description: Attaches a new drop area to the overlay. You may need to release via DragDrop.Release later, especially when switching layouts.

parameters.name: OverlayRef
parameters.description: The overlay window reference number.

functions.result: Returns overlay reference number or error.

examples.label: Create an overlay to accept files by drag and drop:
examples.text_code:
# we create the overlay first
Set Variable [
$overlay
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.Create
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetFrame
";
$overlay
; 100; 100; 200; 200) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetImage
";
$overlay
;
DragTest::DropLogo
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetVisible
";
$overlay
; 1) ]
# we allow user to move the logo
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Overlay.SetMovableByWindowBackground
";
$overlay
; 1) ]
# now add our drop zone to the overlay
Set Variable [
$$dropview
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.AttachToOverlay";
$overlay
) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes
";
$$dropview
; "file,rtf,text,html") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler
";
$$dropview
; "DragTest.fmp12"; "DragAction") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetCursor
";
$$dropview
; 1) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Overlay.Create") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Overlay.SetImage"; $overlay; DragTest::DropLogo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Overlay.SetVisible"; $overlay; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> we allow user to move the logo</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Overlay.SetMovableByWindowBackground"; $overlay; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now add our drop zone to the overlay</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.AttachToOverlay"; $overlay) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$dropview</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes"; $$dropview; "file,rtf,text,html") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler"; $$dropview; "DragTest.fmp12"; "DragAction") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetCursor"; $$dropview; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.AttachToWindow
functions.description: Attaches a new drop area to the window. This disables FileMaker's drop handler and installs the plugin one to the window. You can't assign a picture or change position on Windows. On MacOS calls DragDrop.CreateWithWindow internally. You may need to release via DragDrop.Release later, especially when switching layouts. Be sure to release this when leaving layout or switching to a different window mode. Otherwise you can't drag & drop controls within the layout editor.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

functions.result: Returns overlay reference number or error.

examples.label: Attach to window of the FileMaker solution:
examples.text_code:
# SetupDrop attach in file DragTest drag
# let FileMaker build the layout
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
# now add our control
Set Variable [
$$dropview
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.AttachToWindow"; Get(WindowName) ) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes
";
$$dropview
; "file") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler
";
$$dropview
; Get ( FileName ); "DragAction") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetCursor
";
$$dropview
; 1) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> SetupDrop attach in file DragTest drag</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> let FileMaker build the layout</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now add our control</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.AttachToWindow"; Get(WindowName) ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$dropview</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes"; $$dropview; "file") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler"; $$dropview; Get ( FileName ); "DragAction") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetCursor"; $$dropview; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.CreateWithControl
functions.description: Creates a new drop area on the given window covering the area of the given control. Use DragDrop.Release later to destroy the drop area. If you use a register on the window, be aware that the drop area actually is in front of the register. So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should get the dropped files. Drag & Drop reference numbers are starting at 31000 and counting up for each new drop area. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1 for FileMaker 16 or newer. When creating on a card window, please pass reference to parent window or just 0 for front window. On MacOS you may need to pass delta y value to adjust position. If you create a control on a new window, you may do window update command in your script first to have FileMaker actually build the window with controls and display it. A short script pause or using FM.RunScriptLater with 0.1 delay can help having all controls draw themselves. Then you can use this command to put our control on top. We use GetLayoutObjectAttribute() to query left, top, width and height and this gives empty value if control is not yet here. Creation fails to pick the right window in case the window is selected by name and the window name is not unique. In that case better work with window reference numbers or just pass 0 for current front window.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

functions.result: Returns Drop Area ID on success.

examples.label: Creates a new drop area:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$$dropview
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.CreateWithControl"; Get(WindowName); "drop") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes
";
$$dropview
; "file") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler
";
$$dropview
; Get ( FileName ); "DragAction") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetCursor
";
$$dropview
; 1) ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetImage
";
$$dropview
;
DragTest::DropLogo
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.CreateWithControl"; Get(WindowName); "drop") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$dropview</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes"; $$dropview; "file") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler"; $$dropview; Get ( FileName ); "DragAction") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetCursor"; $$dropview; 1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetImage"; $$dropview; DragTest::DropLogo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.CreateWithSize
functions.signature: MBS( "DragDrop.CreateWithSize"; WindowRef; X; Y; W; H )
functions.description: Creates a new drop area inside the window with the given rectangle. Use DragDrop.Release later to destroy the drop area. If you use a register on the window, be aware that the drop area actually is in front of the register. So you need to check in the script which register page is current and should get the dropped files. Drag & Drop reference numbers are starting at 31000 and counting up for each new drop area. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1 for FileMaker 16 or newer.

parameters.name: WindowRef
parameters.description: Window Reference is the unique OS level window ID. You can obtain this by using the Window.FindByTitle or Window.FindByIndex functions. Pass zero to access the frontmost window.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The left coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The top coordinate in the window.

parameters.name: W
parameters.description: The width of the drop area in the window.

parameters.name: H
parameters.description: The height of the drop area in the window.

functions.result: Returns Drop Area ID on success.

examples.label: Create from Size:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$DragDrop
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.CreateWithSize"; 0; 30; 30; 700; 700 ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.CreateWithSize"; 0; 30; 30; 700; 700 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DragDrop</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetAutoResizingMask
functions.description: Queries auto resizing mask. This defines whether view changes its size based on the surrounding window.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetEmailAccount
functions.description: Returns account name of the specified email message. Index from 0 to DragDrop.GetEmailCount -1. This is metadata provided by Apple Mail. Alternatively you can get more if you take the email source and use our EmailParser functions to read the email file.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Optiona, zero based index of the received email. Default is 0.

examples.label: Query account of last email drop:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.GetEmailAccount"; $$dropview; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$account</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Email account" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$account </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetEmailCount
functions.description: Returns number of emails received from Apple Mail app. If multiple emails arrive, the order of the email metadata (subject, mailbox, account) is not in the same order as the files. So you may prefer to use EmailParser functions to read the subject from each file.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

functions.result: The number of emails or error.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetEmailID
functions.description: Returns subject of the specified email message. Index from 0 to DragDrop.GetEmailCount -1. Using the ID you can ask Mail by Apple Script for the content or other details on the email. For an email drag from Apple Mail, the function DragDrop.GetPath may give the path to a copy of the email file in temporary folder. You can use DragDrop.GetPath to get the email as file.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Optiona, zero based index of the received email. Default is 0.

functions.result: Returns the email id as text.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetEmailMailbox

parameters.name: droparea

parameters.name: Index

functions.name: DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor
functions.description: Queries file descriptor. The file descriptor may include a file path and/or file data. The data is returned as container value. A Drag may include both a normal path and a file descriptor. So you may want to filter and not take both if they reference same file path.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the file descriptor from 0 to DragDrop.GetFileDescriptorCount -1.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Which value to return. Can be Name, Size, Data or Path.

examples.label: Check for a file descriptor coming with Drag & Drop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
("
DragDrop.GetFileDescriptorCount
";
$dropview
; "") ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor";
$dropview
;
$index
; "name") ]
Set Variable [
$data
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor";
$dropview
;
$index
; "data") ]
Set Variable [
$size
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor";
$dropview
;
$index
; "size") ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor";
$dropview
;
$index
; "path") ]
If [ Length (
$path
) > 0 ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
DragTest::field
;
MBS
("
Files.ReadFile
";
$path
; "auto") ]
Set Field [
DragTest::Description
; "Got via file descriptor with path" ]
Set Field [
DragTest::Text
;
$path
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Else If [
$size
> 0 ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
DragTest::field
;
$data
]
Set Field [
DragTest::Text
;
$name
]
Set Field [
DragTest::Description
; "Got via file descriptor with data" ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptorCount"; $dropview; "") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor"; $dropview; $index; "name") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor"; $dropview; $index; "data") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$data</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor"; $dropview; $index; "size") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$size</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor"; $dropview; $index; "path") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $path ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Files.ReadFile"; $path; "auto") </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="field "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Got via file descriptor with path" </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$path </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $size > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$data </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="field "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$name </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Got via file descriptor with data" </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetHTML
functions.description: Queries styled text from drag as HTML. HTML may be prefixed on Windows with metadata like the source URL. You may remove all text before <html> to get only the html.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is Windows ANSI. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

examples.label: Query HTML text:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetHTML";
$dropview
) ]
If [ Length(
$text
) > 0 ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
DragTest::Text
;
$text
]
Set Field [
DragTest::Description
; "Got HTML" ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetHTML"; $dropview) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length($text) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$text </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Got HTML" </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetMouseX
functions.description: Queries the native mouse position where the item was dropped. Call this function in the drag action script. The mouse position is relative to the window. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

examples.label: Queries Mouse X and Y coordinates:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.GetMouseX"; $droparea ) & " / " & MBS( "DragDrop.GetMouseY"; $droparea )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetPath
functions.description: Queries the native path of a file drop. Call this function in the drag action script. For an email drag from Apple Mail, this function may give the path to a copy of the email file in temporary folder. Use Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath to convert to a FileMaker path if you plan to use "filemac:" for an insert file script step. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1. If a picture is dropped and it is not a file, but a picture itself, check DragDrop.GetPicture function for it. On Windows, drops may not include file as paths, but a file descriptor. You need to use DragDrop.GetFileDescriptor function to query data.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Optional index for the file path. Index goes from 0 to DragDrop.GetPathCount -1.

examples.label: Process paths in Drag & Drop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
("
DragDrop.GetPathCount
";
$dropview
) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetPath";
$dropview
;
$index
) ]
If [ Length (
$path
) > 0 ]
If [
MBS
("
Files.FileExists
";
$path
) = 0 ]
# File not yet written? Wait a bit
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): 1 ]
End If
New Record/Request
Set Field [
DragTest::field
;
MBS
("
Files.ReadFile
";
$path
; "auto") ]
Set Field [
DragTest::Text
;
$path
]
Set Field [
DragTest::Description
; "Got via path list" ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetPathCount"; $dropview) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetPath"; $dropview; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $path ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("Files.FileExists"; $path) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> File not yet written? Wait a bit</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("Files.ReadFile"; $path; "auto") </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="field "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$path </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Got via path list" </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Stores files in records:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$dropview
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
("
DragDrop.GetPathCount
";
$dropview
) ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "Number of files: " &
$count
]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$OSPfad
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetPath";
$dropview
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$FMPfad
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath
";
$OSPfad
) ]
#
If [
MBS
("
Files.FileExists
";
$OSPfad
) ]
Go to Layout [ “MIX” ; Animation: None ]
New Record/Request
#
Set Field [
MIX::Path
;
$OSPfad
]
Set Field [
MIX::Name
;
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$OSPfad
) ]
#
Go to Field [
MIX::Image
]
Insert Picture [ Reference ; “
$FMPfad
” ]
#
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Go to Layout [ original layout ; Animation: None ]
End If
#
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:

    <Calculation> MBS("Files.FileExists"; $OSPfad) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="MIX"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$OSPfad </Calculation>
    <Field table="MIX" id="99999" name="Path "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $OSPfad ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MIX" id="99999" name="Name "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="17" name="Go to Field">
    <SelectAll state="False"/>
    <Field table="MIX" id="99999" name="Image"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="56" name="Insert Picture">
    <UniversalPathList type="Reference">$FMPfad</UniversalPathList>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetPathCount
functions.description: Returns the number of file paths. Call this function in the drag action script. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

examples.label: Stores files in records:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$dropview
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetPathCount";
$dropview
) ]
// Show Custom Dialog [ "Number of files: " &
$count
]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$OSPfad
; Value:
MBS
("
DragDrop.GetPath
";
$dropview
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$FMPfad
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath
";
$OSPfad
) ]
#
If [
MBS
("
Files.FileExists
";
$OSPfad
) ]
Go to Layout [ “MIX” ; Animation: None ]
New Record/Request
#
Set Field [
MIX::Path
;
$OSPfad
]
Set Field [
MIX::Name
;
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$OSPfad
) ]
#
Go to Field [
MIX::Image
]
Insert Picture [ Reference ; “
$FMPfad
” ]
#
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Go to Layout [ original layout ; Animation: None ]
End If
#
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$dropview</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $OSPfad ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="MIX" id="99999" name="Name "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="17" name="Go to Field">
    <SelectAll state="False"/>
    <Field table="MIX" id="99999" name="Image"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="56" name="Insert Picture">
    <UniversalPathList type="Reference">$FMPfad</UniversalPathList>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="OriginalLayout"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetPathList
functions.description: Queries the list of file paths. Returns all file paths in the drop area from the last drop operation. Same as DragDrop.GetPath , but return all paths as list.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetPicture
functions.description: Queries picture from drag and drop action. Call this function in the drag action script. This can be a direct picture drag or a file drag where we load the picture file. Added Windows support for version 10.3 to get JPEG, PNG and GIF. The type depends on content, so image type and index parameters are ignored there.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index for the file path. Index goes from 0 to DragDrop.GetPathCount -1.

examples.label: Queries picture as PNG
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DragDrop.GetPicture";
$droparea
; "PNG"; "myfile.png" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.GetPicture"; $droparea; "PNG"; "myfile.png" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetRTF
functions.description: Queries styled text from clipboard as RTF.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is Windows ANSI. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: DragDrop.GetSVG
functions.description: Queries SVG data. e.g. drop SVG image from WebViewer to drop area on MacOS.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name to use. By default we take it from the URL.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetTag
functions.description: Queries the tag string for this dropview.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

functions.result: Returns the stored tag value or an error.

functions.name: DragDrop.GetText
functions.description: Queries text from the drop. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

examples.label: Process text coming with Drag & Drop:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
("DragDrop.GetText";
$dropview
) ]
If [ Length(
$text
) > 0 ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
DragTest::Text
;
$text
]
Set Field [
DragTest::Description
; "Got text" ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DragDrop.GetText"; $dropview) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length($text) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$text </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Text "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"Got text" </Calculation>
    <Field table="DragTest" id="99999" name="Description "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetTop
functions.description: Queries current position of the drop area. As Cocoa framework uses zero for the bottom coordinate, we have to swap for the normal topdown coordinates to be used.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: swap
parameters.description: Whether to swap y coordinate. By default we swap them. Pass 0 to disable or 1 to swap.

examples.label: "Query current positon:"
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$x
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.GetLeft
";
$$dropview
) ]
Set Variable [
$y
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.GetTop";
$$dropview
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Current position" ;
$x
& " x " &
$y
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.GetLeft"; $$dropview ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$x</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.GetTop"; $$dropview ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$y</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Current position" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$x & " x " & $y </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.GetWindowRef

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

functions.result: Returns window reference number or error.

functions.name: DragDrop.List
functions.description: Queries list of all currently active drop areas. This helps debugging, especially finding scripts which do not release their objects properly.

examples.label: List all object IDs:

$list
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.List" ) ]

functions.name: DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes
functions.description: Defines what file types should be accepted for drag and drop for this window. Call this function for each window where you want to define drag types. As FileMaker defines some types by itself, you don't need to call this method for every type. Version 2.7 adds "Emails" as type. You can receive Email drops from Apple Mail this way. The functions DragDrop.GetEmailCount , DragDrop.GetEmailID , DragDrop.GetEmailMailbox , DragDrop.GetEmailAccount and DragDrop.GetEmailSubject give you the details on received emails. GetURL gives URL of email if you received only one. For Version 9.2 or newer you can pass RTF and HTML for the types.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Types
parameters.description: The types you want to accept for the window. This can be Cocoa native type specifications or some special types: "images" for all image types, "movies" for movie types, "file" for files, "URL" for URLs, "Email" for emails and "text" for text. If you use more than one type, separate them with comma.

examples.label: Registers for file drop for the current window:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes";
$droparea
; "file")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes"; $droparea; "file")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$dropview
; Value:
MBS
("
DragDrop.CreateWithControl
"; 0; "DropArea") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.RegisterDropTypes";
$dropview
; "file") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler
";
$dropview
; Get ( FileName ); "DragAction") ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "
DragDrop.SetCursor
";
$dropview
; 1) ]
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

functions.name: DragDrop.Release
functions.description: Releases the drop area and reclaims memory. If you go to layout editor and drop zones are still active, this may cause trouble. Like you can't drag & drop controls.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

examples.label: Releases a drop area:
examples.text_code:
If [ Length(
$$dropView
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.Release";
$$dropview
) ]
Set Variable [
$$dropview
; Value: "" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ Length($$dropView) > 0 ] 
	Set Variable [ $result ; Value: MBS( "DragDrop.Release"; $$dropview) ] 
	Set Variable [ $$dropview ; Value: "" ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.ReleaseAll
functions.description: A convenience method to release all drop areas.
functions.result: Returns number of drop views released.

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.ReleaseAll" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler
functions.description: Sets the drag action handler. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a drag operation drops something on the window. This gives you the possibility to process the data. The script parameter will be the drop area reference. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag action handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler";
$dropHandle
; Get ( FileName ); "MyDragAction")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragActionHandler"; $dropHandle; Get ( FileName ); "MyDragAction")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetDragEnterHandler
functions.description: Sets the drag enter handler. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a drag operation enters the window. This gives you the possibility to draw something like a note "Drop here". The script parameter will be the drop area reference. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag enter handler:

MBS
( "DragDrop.SetDragEnterHandler";
$dropHandle
; Get ( FileName ); "MyDragEnter")

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
