    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetDragExitHandler
functions.description: Sets the drag exit handler. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a drag operation leaves the window. This gives you the possibility to restore the window from anything you drawn there on Drag Enter. The script parameter will be the drop area reference. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag exit handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "DragDrop.SetDragExitHandler";
$dropHandle
; Get ( FileName ); "MyDragExit")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragExitHandler"; $dropHandle; Get ( FileName ); "MyDragExit")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetDragImage
functions.description: Sets the image to use for dragging. This image represents the content of the drag operation. If none is set, we use the image shown in the container. On Windows the image is only shown if the underlaying software supports it. That means it won't show if over some of the FileMaker controls!

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: the image to use. For best results, pass a container value with a PNG image.

examples.label: Set the drag image:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetDragImage"; $$dropview; DragTest::DragImage) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetFilePathsToDrag
functions.description: Sets file path list for drag. If you use this function, the Drop Area can be used to drag files. Not working with DragDrop.AttachToWindow as we need a control to catch mouse events. You may want to write some container file to a temp file in a temp folder and pass path to the file here.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: Paths
parameters.description: List of native file paths.

examples.label: Write temp file and allow dragging it:
examples.text_code:
# find a temp file path
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" ); "export.jpg") ]
# write our container there
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.WriteFile
";
DragTest::GImage
;
$path
) ]
# and use it for dragging
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.SetFilePathsToDrag";
$$dropview
;
$path
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find a temp file path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ); "export.jpg") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> write our container there</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; DragTest::GImage; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and use it for dragging</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetFilePathsToDrag"; $$dropview; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetFrame
functions.signature: MBS( "DragDrop.SetFrame"; droparea; X; Y; Width; Height )
functions.description: Sets a new frame for the drop area. As Cocoa framework uses zero for the bottom coordinate, we have to swap for the normal topdown coordinates to be used.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: X
parameters.description: The x coordinate.

parameters.name: Y
parameters.description: The y coordinate.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: The width of the control.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: The height of the control.

functions.name: DragDrop.SetFrameWithControl
functions.description: Repositions drop area to given control. If you moved control in layout mode, this can move the drop area back to the new position. Also useful to switch between different locations on the layout. Can be used with OnLayoutSizeChange script trigger to reposition control whenever the window changes size.

parameters.name: droparea
parameters.description: The reference ID for this drop area.

parameters.name: ControlName
parameters.description: The name of the control on your FileMaker form. e.g. you can place a rectangle there to define the position.

parameters.name: DX
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to left or right.

parameters.name: DY
parameters.description: A delta to move drop area more to top or bottom.

examples.label: Move drop area:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DragDrop.SetFrameWithControl";
$$dropview
; "drop") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DragDrop.SetFrameWithControl"; $$dropview; "drop") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DragDrop.SetImage
functions.description: Sets the image to be shown in the drop area. Currently not working right in FileMaker 13. Better just put image in layout where you plan to put the drag area. Added Windows support for MBS Plugin 8.1. Please use picture without alpha channel. Image is scaled to viewing area, so best make sure you have exactly two times the pixels in the image relative to size of the control.

parameters.name: droparea

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: the image to use. For best results, pass a container value with a PNG image.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.BatteryLevel
functions.description: The battery charge level for the device. Battery level ranges from 0.0 (fully discharged) to 1.0 (100% charged). Before accessing this property, ensure that battery monitoring is enabled. If battery monitoring is not enabled, battery state is Unknown and the value of this property is -1.0.

examples.label: Show battery level:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Battery Level" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSDevice.BatteryLevel" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.BatteryState
functions.description: The battery state for the device. Value is Unknown, Unplugged, Charging or Full.

functions.name: iOSDevice.IdentifierForVendor
functions.description: An alphanumeric string that uniquely identifies a device to the app’s vendor. The value of this property is the same for apps that come from the same vendor running on the same device. A different value is returned for apps on the same device that come from different vendors, and for apps on different devices regardless of vendor.

examples.label: Show identifier:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Identifier" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSDevice.IdentifierForVendor" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.IsBatteryMonitoringEnabled
functions.description: Checks whether battery monitoring is enabled. Returns 1 if enabled or 0 if disabled.

functions.name: iOSDevice.LocalizedModel
functions.description: The model of the device as a localized string. The value of this property is a string that contains a localized version of the string returned from iOSDevice.Model .

functions.name: iOSDevice.Model
functions.description: The model of the device. Possible examples of model strings are "iPhone" and "iPod touch".

examples.label: Show model:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.Name
functions.description: The name identifying the device. The value of this property is an arbitrary alphanumeric string that is associated with the device as an identifier. For example, you can find the name of an iOS device in the General > About settings. In newer iOS versions, this returns a generic name to keep the real name private to the owner.

examples.label: Show name:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Name" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSDevice.Name" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.Orientation
functions.description: Returns the physical orientation of the device. The value of the property is a constant that indicates the current orientation of the device. This value represents the physical orientation of the device and may be different from the current orientation of your application’s user interface. Value can be Portrait, PortraitUpsideDown, LandscapeLeft, LandscapeRight, FaceUp, FaceDown or Unknown.

functions.name: iOSDevice.OrientationIsLandscape
functions.description: Checks whether orientation is landscape. Returns 1 if orientation is either LandscapeLeft or LandscapeRight. iOSDevice.OrientationIsLandscape and iOSDevice.OrientationIsPortrait may both return zero, if the device is lying flat on a desk.

functions.name: iOSDevice.OrientationIsPortrait
functions.description: Checks whether orientation is portrait. Returns 1 if orientation is either Portrait or PortraitUpsideDown. iOSDevice.OrientationIsLandscape and iOSDevice.OrientationIsPortrait may both return zero, if the device is lying flat on a desk.

functions.name: iOSDevice.SetBatteryMonitoringEnabled
functions.description: Changes battery monitoring state. Enable battery monitoring if your app needs to be notified of changes to the battery state, or if you want to check the battery charge level. The default value of this property is 0, which: Disables the posting of battery-related notifications Disables the ability to read battery charge level and battery state

parameters.name: State
parameters.description: The new state of monitoring. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

examples.label: Enable battery monitoring:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "iOSDevice.SetBatteryMonitoringEnabled"; 1 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "iOSDevice.SetBatteryMonitoringEnabled"; 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: iOSDevice.SystemName

functions.name: iOSDevice.SystemVersion
functions.description: The current version of the operating system. An example of the system version is @"1.2".

examples.label: Show system version:

functions.name: iOSDevice.UserInterfaceIdiom
functions.description: The style of interface to use on the current device. For universal applications, you can use this property to tailor the behavior of your application for a specific type of device. For example, iPhone and iPad devices have different screen sizes, so you might want to create different views and controls based on the type of the current device. Value is Phone, Pad, TV or CarPlay. FileMaker does not support all of those. This function is free to use. Created 18th March 2017 , last changed 18th March 2017 iOSDevice.SystemVersion - iOSKeyboard.GetDidHideEvaluate 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: Is64bit
functions.description: Checks if platform is 64bit or 32bit. Returns 1 for 64bit (e.g. FileMaker Server 13) or 0 for 32 bit (FileMaker Pro 13) Returns 1 on FileMaker Cloud as this uses a 64-bit Linux. See SystemInfo.Is64bitWindows to check if you are 32-bit Windows app on a 64-bit Windows OS.

functions.name: IsRuntime
functions.description: Checks if plugin is running on a runtime. Returns 0 if running on FileMaker Pro or Server. See also IsRegistered , IsServer and IsClient functions.

examples.label: Run something only in runtime:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "IsRuntime" )]
# runs on runtime
Else
# runs on non runtime or where no plugin is installed
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS( "IsRuntime" )]
	# runs on runtime
Else
	# runs on non runtime or where no plugin is installed
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: IsServer
functions.description: Checks if plugin is running on a server. So if you see 0 returned, your script or calculation is not performed on a server, but on a client. Should return 1 always for scheduled server scripts or web direct scripts. See also IsRegistered , IsClient and IsRuntime .

examples.label: Run a script on server:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
("IsServer")]
# We run on server already
Else
# Call this script on server
Perform Script on Server [Wait for completion; “Your Script”; Parameter: Get(ScriptParameter)]
If [Get(LastError)  ≠ 0]
# Can't run on server, e.g. too many connections.
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS("IsServer")]
	# We run on server already
Else
	# Call this script on server
	Perform Script on Server [Wait for completion; “Your Script”; Parameter: Get(ScriptParameter)]
	If [Get(LastError)  ≠ 0]
		# Can't run on server, e.g. too many connections.
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Perform Script on Server:
examples.text_code:
# are we on Server?
If [
MBS
("IsServer") = 0]
# perform same script on server and pass parameter
Perform Script on Server [Wait for completion; “Do Something on Server”; Parameter: Get ( ScriptParameter )]
# check error and result
Set Variable [
$LastError
; Value:Get ( LastError )]
# Maybe handle here error code if server is too busy and decide what to do, e.g. run script locally?
If [
$LastError
≠ 0]
# run locally by fall through
# alternative: Exit script here and return error to caller
Else
Set Variable [
$ScriptResult
; Value:Get ( ScriptResult )]
#Return result back to caller
Exit Script [Result:
$ScriptResult
]
End If
End If
#This now performs on Server or on Client if Server is too busy
Go to Layout [“Test”]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
perform::First
; 1]
Set Field [
perform::Last
; 2]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
# end of script
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> are we on Server?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsServer") = 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> perform same script on server and pass parameter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="164" name="Perform Script on Server">
    <WaitForCompletion state="True"/>
    <Calculation>Get ( ScriptParameter )</Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Do Something on Server"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> check error and result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get ( LastError )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LastError</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Maybe handle here error code if server is too busy and decide what to do, e.g. run script locally?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$LastError ≠ 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> run locally by fall through</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> alternative: Exit script here and return error to caller</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get ( ScriptResult )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ScriptResult</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Return result back to caller</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation>Result: $ScriptResult</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>This now performs on Server or on Client if Server is too busy</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="Test"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    <Field table="perform" id="99999" name="First"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>2</Calculation>
    <Field table="perform" id="99999" name="Last"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> end of script</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: LCMS.ProfileInfo
functions.description: Queries profile information for an ICC Profile. The name field is dynamically build from Model, Manufacturer and/or Description.

parameters.name: ProfileData
parameters.description: The container containing the ICC profile as file.

parameters.name: Selector
parameters.description: Currently can be Name, Description, Copyright, Model, Manufacturer.

examples.label: Shows various informations:
examples.text_code:
Show Custom Dialog ["Info";
"Name: " &
MBS
("LCMS.PRofileInfo";
Drawing::ICCProfile
; "Name") & ¶ &
"Model: " &
MBS
("LCMS.PRofileInfo";
Drawing::ICCProfile
; "Model") & ¶ &
"Manufacturer: " &
MBS
("LCMS.PRofileInfo";
Drawing::ICCProfile
; "Manufacturer") & ¶ &
"Description: " &
MBS
("LCMS.PRofileInfo";
Drawing::ICCProfile
; "Description") & ¶ &
"Copyright: " &
MBS
("LCMS.PRofileInfo";
Drawing::ICCProfile
; "Copyright")]
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"Copyright")</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Show debugger from within a script:
examples.text_code:
#The menu command as we got it from
Menubar.ListMenuCommands
Set Variable [
$DebuggerMenuID
; Value:49260]
If [
MBS
( "
Menubar.HasMenuCommand
";
$DebuggerMenuID
) = 1]
If [
MBS
( "Menubar.IsMenuCommandChecked";
$DebuggerMenuID
) = 1]
#debugger already visible
Else
#show debugger
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Menubar.RunMenuCommand
";
$DebuggerMenuID
)]
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
End If
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["Debugger missing in Menubar?"]
End If
#now follows your script that should always run in debugger
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>The menu command as we got it from Menubar.ListMenuCommands</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>49260</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DebuggerMenuID</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS( "Menubar.HasMenuCommand"; $DebuggerMenuID ) = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS( "Menubar.IsMenuCommandChecked"; $DebuggerMenuID ) = 1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>debugger already visible</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>show debugger</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Menubar.RunMenuCommand"; $DebuggerMenuID )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Debugger missing in Menubar?"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>now follows your script that should always run in debugger</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Menubar.ListMenuCommands
functions.description: Lists all menu commands. The result is a list with a line for each menu entry. The data is separated with tab character. First in each row is the command ID followed by the menu item names for the path to the item. Works well in FileMaker 12 or newer, but not in FileMaker 11.

("Menubar.ListMenuCommands")

functions.name: Menubar.RunMenuCommand
functions.description: Runs a menu command. The plugin searches menu command and invokes it. For Windows, the command is run asynchronously, so the function returns before the command runs. You may need to make a script pause to run the command. For a couple of menu commands you may need to use Schedule.EvaluateAfterDelay to make sure the plugin invokes the menu after the script finished running.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The ID or path to the menu entry. Use Menubar.ListMenuCommands to find out the IDs and names.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.description: for macOS an optional delay to run the command after seconds. e.g. pass 0.1 to run command after 100ms.

examples.label: Run command to select all:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("Menubar.RunMenuCommand"; "Bearbeiten¶Alles auswählen")

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set Shortcut to "⌘⇧D" for the Data Viewer by ID:
examples.text_code:
MBS

functions.name: Menubar.SetMenuCommandVisible
functions.description: Show or hide a menu command. This allows you to remove menu entries by path/ID from the FileMaker menu, so user cannot longer use it. The change is not permanent and whenever FileMaker rebuilds the menu, all menu entries are back. On Windows we can only remove the entry, so we do so if you pass visible = 0.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The ID or path to the menu entry. Use Menubar.ListMenuCommands to find out the IDs and names.

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: The new visible state. Pass 1 to make visible and 0 to hide.

functions.name: MongoDB.AbortTransaction
functions.description: Abort a multi-document transaction. Returns OK if the transaction was aborted. Returns an error if there are invalid arguments, such as a session with no transaction in progress. Network or server errors are ignored.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

examples.label: Try aborting:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Open connection first, open database and connection</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Estimate before" ; MBS( "MongoDB.EstimatedDocumentCount"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$mongo ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.StartTransaction"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> insert a record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.InsertOne"; $Mongo; test::Insert JSON) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Estimate after insert" ; MBS( "MongoDB.EstimatedDocumentCount"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$mongo ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.AbortTransaction"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Estimate after abort" ; MBS( "MongoDB.EstimatedDocumentCount"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$mongo ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.AggregateCollection
functions.description: Creates an aggregate collection. This function creates a cursor which sends the aggregate command on the underlying collection upon the first call to MongoDB.CursorNext . For more information on building aggregation pipelines, see the MongoDB Manual entry on the aggregate command. Sets the current cursor. Please review the documentation for MongoDB on how to build the JSON for pipeline or options.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags.

parameters.name: Pipeline
parameters.description: The pipeline as JSON.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: JSON with options.

functions.name: MongoDB.AggregateDatabase
functions.description: Creates an aggregate database. This function creates a cursor which sends the aggregate command on the underlying database upon the first call to MongoDB.CursorNext . For more information on building aggregation pipelines, see the MongoDB Manual entry on the aggregate command. Note that the pipeline must start with a compatible stage that does not require an underlying collection (e.g. "$currentOp", "$listLocalSessions"). Sets the current cursor. Please review the documentation for MongoDB on how to build the JSON for pipeline or options.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Pipeline
parameters.description: The pipeline as JSON.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: JSON with options.

examples.label: List local sessions:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.AggregateDatabase";
$Mongo
; "[{\"
$listLocalSessions
\": {}}]") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.CursorNext
";
$Mongo
) ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") // some error like connection dropped ]
Exit Loop If [ Length (
$json
) = 0 // end of cursor ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Record" ;
$json
]
Exit Loop If [ Get(LastMessageChoice) = 2 ]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.AggregateDatabase"; $Mongo; "[{\"$listLocalSessions\": {}}]") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.CursorNext"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") // some error like connection dropped </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Length ( $json ) = 0 // end of cursor </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Record" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Get(LastMessageChoice) = 2 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.ChangeStreamErrorDocument
functions.description: Checks if an error has occurred when creating or iterating over a change stream. Similar to MongoDB.CursorErrorDocument if the error has occurred client-side then the reply will be set to an empty BSON document. If the error occurred server-side, reply is set to the server’s reply document.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.ChangeStreamResumeToken
functions.description: Queries resume token. Returns the cached resume token, which may be passed as either the resumeAfter or startAfter option of a watch function to start a new change stream from the same point. Returns "" if no resume token is available. This is possible if the change stream has not been iterated and neither resumeAfter nor startAfter options were specified in the watch function.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.ClientCommand
functions.description: Execute a command on the server, interpreting options according to the MongoDB server version. To send a raw command to the server without any of this logic, use MongoDB.Command . Read preferences, read and write concern, and collation can be overridden by various sources.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: dbName
parameters.description: The name of the database.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command as JSON.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: JSON with options.

functions.name: MongoDB.ClientCommandWithServerId
functions.description: This function executes a command on a specific server, using the database and command specification provided. This function is not considered a retry-able read operation.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: dbName
parameters.description: The name of the database to run the command on.

parameters.name: Query
parameters.description: The query as JSON.

parameters.name: ServerID
parameters.description: An opaque id specifying which server to use.

functions.name: MongoDB.CloseChangeStream
functions.description: Closes change stream. This function is optional to close the current change stream. Normally it is freed when a new one is set or the MongoDB.Release function is called. This function returns OK whether a change stream is there or not.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CloseCollection
functions.description: Closes the current collection explicitly. You usually don't need to call this as this happens automatically when you open a new collection or close the connection or database.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CloseCursor
functions.description: Closes cursor. This function is optional to close the current cursor. Normally it is freed when a new one is set or the MongoDB.Release function is called. This function returns OK whether a cursor is there or not.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.Command
functions.description: Execute a command on the server. Please open relevant collection first with MongoDB.OpenCollection . Consult the MongoDB Manual entry on Database Commands for each command’s arguments.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The command as JSON.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: JSON with options.

examples.label: Run Hello command:
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: MongoDB.CommitTransaction
functions.description: Commit a multi-document transaction. Returns JSON if the transaction was committed. Raises exception if there are invalid arguments, such as a session with no transaction in progress, or if there is a server or network error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.Connect
functions.description: Connects the client using the URI provided. See connection string examples in MongoDB.SetURI function.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: DoPing
parameters.description: Whether to ping server and make a connection. Default is 1 to enable.

examples.label: Connect:
examples.text_code:
# start a new session
Set Variable [
$Mongo
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.New
" ) ]
# where is the server?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/" ) ]
# connect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.Connect";
$Mongo
) ]
#
# now do something
#
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]

examples.label: Run SSH Tunnel to connect to MongoDB:
examples.text_code:
# some settings like local IP/Port
Set Variable [
$LocalIP
; Value: "
127.0
.
0.1
" ]
Set Variable [
$LocalPort
; Value: 12345 ]
# the SSH server to connect through
Set Variable [
$SSHServerIP
; Value: "myserver.test" ]
# database to connect
Set Variable [
$DatabaseIP
; Value: "db.myserver.test" ]
Set Variable [
$DatabasePort
; Value: 27017 ]
# create connection
Set Variable [
$ssh
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Connect
";
$ssh
;
$SSHServerIP
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.SessionHandshake
";
$ssh
) ]
# using private key file here, but username+password is also possible
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.UserAuthPublicKeyFile
";
$ssh
; "username"; "" ;
$keypair_path
; "password" ) ]
Set Variable [
$auth
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.IsAuthenticated
";
$ssh
) ]
# must be 1
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Run
";
$ssh
;
$DatabaseIP
;
$DatabasePort
;
$LocalIP
;
$LocalPort
) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .5 ]
Set Variable [
$tunnel_mess
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Messages
";
$ssh
) ]
# now this should show that tunnel is waiting.
Set Variable [
$MongoDB
; Value:
MBS
("
MongoDB.New
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$MongoDB
; "mongodb://" &
$LocalIP
& ":" &
$DatabasePort
& "/") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("MongoDB.Connect";
$MongoDB
) ]
Set Variable [
$tunnel_mess
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Messages
";
$ssh
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [
$tunnel_mess
&  ¶ &
$r
]
# should show OK for connect and for tunnel that it's forwarding data
# do query here
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.DatabasesNames
";
$MongoDB
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Databases" ;
$list
]
# disconnect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("SQL.Release";
$MongoDB
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Tunnel.Cancel
";
$ssh
) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): .1 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Disconnect
";
$ssh
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
SSH.Release
";
$ssh
) ]

functions.name: MongoDB.ContainerToBinary
functions.description: Converts a container to JSON for MongoDB. Reads the container, converts to base64 and embeds it in a $binary JSON for use in MongoDB.

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.CreateCollection
functions.description: Creates a collection in the database. If no write concern is provided in opts, the database’s write concern is used. For a list of all options, see the MongoDB Manual entry on the create command. Raises an exception in case of an error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The new collection name.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorBatchSize
functions.description: Queries batch size. Retrieve the cursor’s batch size, the maximum number of documents returned per round trip to the server. A batch size of zero means the cursor accepts the server’s maximum batch size.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorCurrent
functions.description: Queries current record as JSON. Fetches the cursors current document or „" if there has been an error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorErrorDocument
functions.description: Checks to see if an error has occurred while iterating the cursor. If an error occurred server-side, the result is set to the server’s reply document with information about the error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.result: Returns empty result, JSON or error.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorGetMaxAwaitTimeMS
functions.description: The maximum amount of time for the server to wait on new documents to satisfy a tailable cursor query.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorID
functions.description: Retrieves the cursor id used by the server to track the cursor. This number is zero until the driver actually uses a server when executing the query, and after it has fetched all results from the server.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorMore
functions.description: Whether there may be more data. This function shall indicate if there is potentially more data to be read from the cursor. This is only useful with tailable cursors. Use NextRecord() for regular cursors. Details: mongoc_cursor_more is unreliable because it does not contact the server to see if there are actually more documents in the result set. It simply returns true if the cursor has not begun, or if it has begun and there are buffered documents in the client-side cursor, or if it has begun and the server has not yet told the cursor it is completely iterated. This is unreliable with regular queries because it returns true for a new cursor before iteration, even if the cursor will match no documents. It is also true if the collection has been dropped on the server since the previous fetch, or if the cursor has finished its final batch and the next batch will be empty. Returns true if the cursor has locally-buffered documents, or if a round-trip to the server might fetch additional documents.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorNext
functions.description: Queries the next record as JSON. This function returns a JSON document was read from the cursor. Otherwise, "" is returned if the cursor was exhausted. And it can return an error in other cases like a dropped connection.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

examples.label: Loop over cursor:
examples.text_code:
# get cursor for records describing collections
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.FindCollections
";
$Mongo
) ]
# loop over records and show each
Loop
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.CursorNext";
$Mongo
) ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") // some error like connection dropped ]
Exit Loop If [ Length (
$json
) = 0 // end of cursor ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Record" ;
$json
]
Exit Loop If [ Get(LastMessageChoice) = 2 // cancel clicked ]
End Loop
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get cursor for records describing collections</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.FindCollections"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over records and show each</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.CursorNext"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") // some error like connection dropped </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Length ( $json ) = 0 // end of cursor </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Record" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> Get(LastMessageChoice) = 2 // cancel clicked </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorRecords
functions.description: Queries the all records as JSON. This function loops and collects all records in a big JSON array. Otherwise, "" is returned if the cursor was exhausted. And it can return an error in other cases like a dropped connection. Returns out of memory error if size of JSON exceeds 2 GB.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorSetBatchSize
functions.description: Sets batch size, Limits the number of documents returned in one batch. Each batch requires a round trip to the server. If the batch size is zero, the cursor uses the server-defined maximum batch size.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Size
parameters.description: The new batch size.

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorSetLimit
functions.description: Limits the number of documents in the result set.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: The new limit.

examples.label: Sets limit and shows it:
examples.text_code:
# first make a find
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.CursorSetLimit";
$Mongo
; 2) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Current cursor limit:" ;
MBS
( "
MongoDB.CursorLimit
";
$mongo
) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> first make a find</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.CursorSetLimit"; $Mongo; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Current cursor limit:" ; MBS( "MongoDB.CursorLimit"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$mongo ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.CursorSetMaxAwaitTimeMS
functions.description: Sets the maximum amount of time for the server to wait on new documents to satisfy a tailable cursor query.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value in milliseconds.

functions.name: MongoDB.DatabaseCommand
functions.description: Creates a collection in the database. If no write concern is provided in opts, the database’s write concern is used. For a list of all options, see the MongoDB Manual entry on the create command. Raises an exception in case of an error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Command
parameters.description: The comand to run as JSON.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Options as JSON.

examples.label: Ping the server:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Mongo
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/" ) ]
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "

";
$Mongo
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenDatabase
";
$Mongo
; "local" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "
MongoDB.OpenCollection
";
$Mongo
; "test" ) ]
# send a ping command

$r
; Value:

$Mongo
; "{\"ping\": 1}") ]

$r
]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]

functions.name: MongoDB.DeleteMany
functions.description: Deletes many records. This function removes all documents in the given collection that match selector. To delete at most one matching document, use MongoDB.DeleteOne .

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: selectorJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing the query to match documents for deleting.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: Options as JSON.

examples.label: Delete several matching documents:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.DeleteMany";
$Mongo
; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}") ]
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.DeleteMany"; $Mongo; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.DeleteOne
functions.description: Deletes one record. This function removes at most one document in the given collection that matches selector. If there are multiple documents (records), the others are ignored. To delete all matching documents, use MongoDB.DeleteMany . Returns string with JSON about errors on server.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: selectorJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing the query to match documents for deleting.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: Options as JSON.

examples.label: Delete one record machting:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.DeleteOne";
$Mongo
; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}") ]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.Find
functions.description: Query on collection, passing arbitrary query options to the server in options. Sets the current cursor. See also find command documented here: https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/command/find/

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: FilterJSON
parameters.description: The JSON with filter details.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: The JSON with options.

examples.label: Find records:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Mongo
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Connect
";
$Mongo
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenDatabase
";
$Mongo
; "local" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenCollection
";
$Mongo
; "test" ) ]
# get cursor for records describing collections
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.Find";
$Mongo
; "{\"Hello\": \"World\"}"; "{\"limit\": 10, \"sort\":{\"Hello\": -1}}") ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to find" ;
$r
]
Else
# loop over records and show each
Loop
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.CursorNext
";
$Mongo
) ]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("IsError") // some error like connection dropped ]
Exit Loop If [ Length (
$json
) = 0 // end of cursor ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Record" ;
$json
]
End Loop
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]

examples.label: Load image from binary data in a record:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.Find";
$Mongo
; "{\"FileID\": 123}") ]
If [
MBS
("ISError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to find" ;
$r
]
Else
# JSON like this: {"Data":{"
$binary
":{"base64":"iVBORw0K...ElFTkSuQmCC\r\n","subType":0}},"FileID":123,"filename":"test.png"}
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.CursorNext
";
$Mongo
) ]
If [ Length (
$json
) > 0 ]
Set Field [
test::Insert
JSON ;
$json
]
Set Field [
test::Image
; Base64Decode ( JSONGetElement (
test::Insert
JSON ; "Data.
$binary
.base64" ); JSONGetElement (
test::Insert
JSON ; "filename" ) ) ]
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:

    </Value>

      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("ISError") </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to find" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> JSON like this: {"Data":{"$binary":{"base64":"iVBORw0K...ElFTkSuQmCC\r\n","subType":0}},"FileID":123,"filename":"test.png"}</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.CursorNext"; $Mongo) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $json ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="Insert JSON "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>Base64Decode ( JSONGetElement ( test::Insert JSON ; "Data.$binary.base64" ); JSONGetElement ( test::Insert JSON ; "filename" ) ) </Calculation>
    <Field table="test" id="99999" name="Image "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.FindCollections

parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: JSON with options.

examples.label: Find records for collections:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Mongo
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Connect
";
$Mongo
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenDatabase
";
$Mongo
; "local" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenCollection
";
$Mongo
; "test" ) ]
# get cursor for records describing collections
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "MongoDB.FindCollections";
$Mongo
) ]
# loop over records and show each
Loop
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:

( "
MongoDB.CursorNext
";
$Mongo
) ]
Exit Loop If [

("IsError") // some error like connection dropped ]

$json
) = 0 // end of cursor ]

$json
]
End Loop
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]

functions.name: MongoDB.HasCollection
functions.description: Checks to see if a collection exists on the MongoDB server within database. If the function succeeds, it returns 1 if the collection exists and 0 if not. Returns an error in case of an invalid arguments or a server or network error.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the collection.

functions.name: MongoDB.InTransaction
functions.description: Check whether a multi-document transaction is in progress for this session. Returns 1 if a transaction was started and has not been committed or aborted, otherwise 0.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

examples.label: Abort transaction if we still have one:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "MongoDB.InTransaction";
$Mongo
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.AbortTransaction
";
$mongo
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "MongoDB.InTransaction"; $Mongo ) ] 
	Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS( "MongoDB.AbortTransaction"; $mongo ) ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.InsertMany
functions.description: Insert documents into collection. Please pass a JSON array as string for all documents. Each entry in the array should be a JSON object with the document data. For any document that does not have an "_id" field, a bson_oid_t will be generated locally and added to the document. If you must know the inserted document's _id, generate it in your code and include it in the document. The _id you generate can be a bson_oid_t or any other non-array BSON type. The reply is filled out with an "insertedCount" field. If there is a server error then reply may contain a "writeErrors" array and/or a "writeConcernErrors" array.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: documentJSON
parameters.description: The JSON with an array of the records to save.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: The options to use.

examples.label: Insert two records in one call:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value: "[{\"ID\": 1, \"Name\": \"John\"}, {\"ID\": 2, \"Name\": \"Miller\"}]" ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.InsertMany";
$Mongo
;
$JSON
) ]
Example result:
{
"insertedCount"
:
2
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"[{\"ID\": 1, \"Name\": \"John\"}, {\"ID\": 2, \"Name\": \"Miller\"}]" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.InsertMany"; $Mongo; $JSON) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.InsertOne
functions.description: Inserts a document into collection. To insert an array of documents, see MongoDB.InsertMany . If no _id element is found in document, then an oid will be generated locally and added to the document. If you must know the inserted document’s _id, generate it in your code and include it in the document. The _id you generate can be an old or any other non-array BSON type. The reply is filled out with an "insertedCount" field. If there is a server error then reply contains either a "writeErrors" array with one subdocument or a "writeConcernErrors" array. OptionsJSON may be empty or a JSON document with additional command options: writeConcern: the write concern. sessionId: The session ID. validate: The validation flags. bypassDocumentValidation: Set to true to skip server-side schema validation of the provided BSON documents. Use MongoDB.InsertMany to insert multiple documents. Binary Data can be passed with {"$binary"} JSON like showing in the example below:

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: documentJSON
parameters.description: The JSON with the record to save.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: The options to use.

examples.label: Insert a Record:
examples.text_code:
# start a new session
Set Variable [
$Mongo
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.New
" ) ]
# where is the server?
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/" ) ]
# connect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Connect
";
$Mongo
) ]
# open the database
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenDatabase
";
$Mongo
; "local" ) ]
# open the collection (table)
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.OpenCollection
";
$Mongo
; "test" ) ]
# now insert a record with passing a JSON
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.InsertOne";
$Mongo
; "{\"Hello\":  \"FileMaker\"}" ) ]
# and show answer/error
Show Custom Dialog [ "Insert Reply" ;
$r
]
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]

$r
; Value:

( "MongoDB.IsTracing" ) ]

$r

parameters.description: Additioanl options. See MongoDB documentation.

functions.name: MongoDB.NextChange
functions.description: Iterates the underlying change stream, returning the next document. This function returns a valid JSON document was read from the stream. Otherwise, if there was an error it returns an error or empty if no document was available.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

examples.label: Query the next change in the queue to check:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.NextChange"; $$MongoDB ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> not IsEmpty ( $$json ) </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Change" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$$json </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.Rename
functions.description: This function is a helper to rename an existing collection on a MongoDB server. The name of the collection will also be updated internally so it is safe to continue using this collection after the rename. Additional operations will occur on renamed collection.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: NewDatabase
parameters.description: The new name for the database.

parameters.name: NewCollectionName
parameters.description: The new name of the collection.

parameters.name: DropTargetBeforeRename
parameters.description: If an existing collection matches the new name, drop it before the rename. Pass 1 to drop or 0 to not drop and return an error in case of a name collision. Default is 0.

functions.name: MongoDB.ReplaceOne
functions.description: This replace one document in the collection that match selector with replacement. Returns the reply JSON is populated with the fields matchedCount, modifiedCount, upsertedCount, and optionally upsertedId if applicable. If there is a server error then reply contains either a writeErrors array with one subdocument or a writeConcernErrors array.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: selectorJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing the query to match the document for updating.

parameters.name: updateJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing the replacement document.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: Options as JSON.

examples.label: Replace one record:

$Mongo
; Value:

MongoDB.New
" ) ]

functions.name: MongoDB.ServerDescriptions
functions.description: Fetches an server descroptions for all known servers in the topology. Returns no servers until the client connects. Returns a single server if the client is directly connected, or all members of a replica set if the client’s MongoDB URI includes a "replicaSet" option, or all known mongos servers if the MongoDB URI includes a list of them.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: A text consisting of one or more comma (,) separated compressors (e.g. “snappy,zlib”) or "". Passing "" clears any existing compressors set on uri.

examples.label: Set compressor to use:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.SetCompressors";
$mongo
; "zlib" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.SetCompressors"; $mongo; "zlib" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.SetDatabase
functions.description: Sets the URI's database, after the URI has been parsed. The driver authenticates to this database if the connection string includes authentication credentials. This database is used when you use MongoDB.OpenDefaultDatabase function.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: DatabaseName
parameters.description: The new database name.

functions.name: MongoDB.SetPassword
functions.description: Sets user password for login. Please call MongoDB.SetURI first to set URL for server before you set more options. Sets the URI’s password, after the URI has been parsed from a string. The driver authenticates with this password if the username is also set.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The new password value.

examples.label: Sets login:

; Value:

$Mongo

; "bob") ]

";

)  & " with password " &

";
$mongo
) ]

functions.name: MongoDB.SetSSLOptions
functions.signature: MBS( "MongoDB.SetSSLOptions"; MongoDBRef; ClientCertificateKeyFile; ClientCertificateKeyPassword; CertificateAuthorityFile { ; CertificateAuthorityDirectory; CertificateRevocationListFile; AllowInvalidCertificates; AllowInvalidHostnames } )
functions.description: Sets the TLS (SSL) options to use when connecting to TLS enabled MongoDB servers. A call to MongoDB.SetSSLOptions overrides all TLS options set through the connection string with which the client was constructed. All texts can be empty if needed. See Files.DeleteLater to delete a temporary key file when FileMaker quits.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: ClientCertificateKeyFile
parameters.description: The native path to the PEM file with the private key to use.

parameters.name: ClientCertificateKeyPassword
parameters.description: The password for the PEM file with private key.

parameters.name: CertificateAuthorityFile
parameters.description: The native file path for a CA file. e.g. get such a file from CURL website: https://curl.se/docs/caextract.html

parameters.name: CertificateAuthorityDirectory
parameters.description: The native file path to the CA directory.

parameters.name: CertificateRevocationListFile
parameters.description: The native file path to the Certificate revocation list file.

parameters.name: AllowInvalidCertificates
parameters.description: Whether to do weaker certificate validation. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default is 0 to disallow.

parameters.name: AllowInvalidHostnames
parameters.description: Whether to allow invalid host names. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default is 0 to disallow. This allows you to disable hostname validation to allow to continue if the hostname doesn't match the certificate.

examples.text_code:

$Mongo
; Value:

( "

" ) ]
If [

("IsError") ]

$Mongo
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]

# set URL with authentication via certificate
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetURI
";
$Mongo
; "mongodb://localhost/?tls=true" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$x
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to parse connection str…" ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]

#
# specify where to find keys
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MongoDB.SetSSLOptions";
$Mongo
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/key.pem"; "test123"; "/Users/cs/Desktop/cacert.pem" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.SetAuthMechanism
";
$Mongo
; "MONGODB-X509" ) ]
#
# now connect
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Connect
";
$Mongo
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Set Variable [
$x
; Value:
MBS
( "
MongoDB.Release
";
$Mongo
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to connect to MongoDB." ;
$r
]
Exit Script [ Text Result:    ]
End If

functions.name: MongoDB.SetURI
functions.description: Sets the URI. Parses a string containing a MongoDB style URI connection string. Returns an error in case of a parsing error. Examples of some valid MongoDB connection strings can be seen below. "mongodb://localhost/" "mongodb://localhost/?replicaSet=myreplset" "mongodb://myuser:mypass@localhost/" "mongodb://kerberosuser%40EXAMPLE.COM@example.com/?authMechanism=GSSAPI" "mongodb://[::1]:27017/" "mongodb://10.0.0.1:27017,10.0.0.1:27018,[::1]:27019/?tls=true" "mongodb://%2Ftmp%2Fmongodb-27017.sock" "mongodb://user:pass@%2Ftmp%2Fmongodb-27017.sock" "mongodb://localhost,[::1]/mydb?authSource=mydb" If you like to use a SSH tunnel, you can create a tunnel with our SSH functions. Use SSH.Tunnel.Run to connect a local port with your remote server and the destination (usually port 27017). Then connect to localhost with the local port.

parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: URI
parameters.description: The URI to use.

functions.name: MongoDB.StartTransaction
functions.description: Start a multi-document transaction for all following operations in this session. The transaction must be completed with CommitTransaction or AbortTransaction. An in-progress transaction is automatically aborted by destructor. Raises exception if there are invalid arguments, such as a session with a transaction already in progress. If you run MongoDB server locally, you may need to configure a replication. Otherwise you get an error like this: "Error 17:20: Transaction numbers are only allowed on a replica set member or mongos".

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

functions.name: MongoDB.UpdateMany
functions.description: Updates many documents in collection that matches selector. To update at most one document see UpdateOne(). The result JSON is filled out with fields matchedCount, modifiedCount, and optionally upsertedId if applicable. If there is a server error then reply contains either a "writeErrors" array with one subdocument or a "writeConcernErrors" array.

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MongoDB.UpdateOne"; $mongo; 
// we select record with ID field
JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; "ID" ; Assets::PrimaryKey ; JSONString );
// and here pass whole record including all fields we like to move
JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; "$set" ; Assets::JSON ; JSONObject );
// and here allow to insert, if record is new
