    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MongoDB.WatchClient
functions.description: Creates a change stream for a client. It is preferred to call this function over using a raw aggregation to create a change stream. This function uses the read preference and read concern of the client. If the change stream needs to re-establish connection, the same read preference will be used. This may happen if the change stream encounters a resumable error. Warning A change stream is only supported with majority read concern. This function is considered a retryable read operation. Upon a transient error (a network error, errors due to replica set failover, etc.) the operation is safely retried once. If retryreads is false in the URI the retry behavior does not apply. Call this method on the client which the change stream listens to. pipelineJSON: A JSON representing an aggregation pipeline appended to the change stream. This may be an empty document. OptionsJSON: A JSON containing change stream options. options may be "" or a JSON document with additional command options: batchSize: An integer representing number of documents requested to be returned on each call to NextChange() resumeAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with startAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. Unlike resumeAfter, this can resume notifications after an "invalidate" event. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAtOperationTime: A Timestamp. The change stream only provides changes that occurred at or after the specified timestamp. Any command run against the server will return an operation time that can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAfter. maxAwaitTimeMS: An int64 representing the maximum amount of time a call to StreamResumeToken() will block waiting for data fullDocument: An optional UTF-8 string. Set this option to "default", "updateLookup", "whenAvailable", or "required", If unset, The string "default" is assumed. Set this option to "updateLookup" to direct the change stream cursor to lookup the most current majority-committed version of the document associated to an update change stream event. fullDocumentBeforeChange: An optional UTF-8 string. Set this option to "whenAvailable", "required", or "off". When unset, the default value is "off". Similar to "fullDocument", but returns the value of the document before the associated change. comment: A JSON specifying the comment to attach to this command. The comment will appear in log messages, profiler output, and currentOp output. Only string values are supported prior to MongoDB 4.4.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: pipelineJSON
parameters.description: A JSON representing an aggregation pipeline appended to the change stream. This may be an empty document.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing change stream options. options may be "" or a JSON document with additional command options.

functions.name: MongoDB.WatchCollection
functions.description: Creates a change stream for a collection. It is preferred to call this function over using a raw aggregation to create a change stream. You can watch what other connections (on other computers) change in the collection (table) and use this change stream to log changes. This allows to have a FileMaker app watch the collection and trigger scripts when something interesting happens, e.g. a new record is created to react or audit. This function uses the read preference and read concern of the collection. If the change stream needs to re-establish connection, the same read preference will be used. This may happen if the change stream encounters a resumable error. Warning A change stream is only supported with majority read concern. This function is considered a retryable read operation. Upon a transient error (a network error, errors due to replica set failover, etc.) the operation is safely retried once. If retryreads is false in the URI the retry behavior does not apply. Call this method on the collection which the change stream listens to. pipelineJSON: A JSON representing an aggregation pipeline appended to the change stream. This may be an empty document. OptionsJSON: A JSON containing change stream options. options may be "" or a JSON document with additional command options: batchSize: An integer representing number of documents requested to be returned on each call to NextChange() resumeAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with startAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. Unlike resumeAfter, this can resume notifications after an "invalidate" event. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAtOperationTime: A Timestamp. The change stream only provides changes that occurred at or after the specified timestamp. Any command run against the server will return an operation time that can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAfter. maxAwaitTimeMS: An int64 representing the maximum amount of time a call to StreamResumeToken() will block waiting for data fullDocument: An optional UTF-8 string. Set this option to "default", "updateLookup", "whenAvailable", or "required", If unset, The string "default" is assumed. Set this option to "updateLookup" to direct the change stream cursor to lookup the most current majority-committed version of the document associated to an update change stream event. fullDocumentBeforeChange: An optional UTF-8 string. Set this option to "whenAvailable", "required", or "off". When unset, the default value is "off". Similar to "fullDocument", but returns the value of the document before the associated change. comment: A JSON specifying the comment to attach to this command. The comment will appear in log messages, profiler output, and currentOp output. Only string values are supported prior to MongoDB 4.4.

parameters.name: MongoDBRef
parameters.description: The reference number for the mongo connection.

parameters.name: pipelineJSON
parameters.description: A JSON representing an aggregation pipeline appended to the change stream. This may be an empty document.

parameters.name: OptionsJSON
parameters.description: A JSON containing change stream options. options may be "" or a JSON document with additional command options.

functions.name: MongoDB.WatchDatabase

You can watch what other connections (on other computers) change in the database and use this change stream to log changes. This function uses the read preference and read concern of the database. If the change stream needs to re-establish connection, the same read preference will be used. This may happen if the change stream encounters a resumable error. Warning A change stream is only supported with majority read concern. This function is considered a retryable read operation. Upon a transient error (a network error, errors due to replica set failover, etc.) the operation is safely retried once. If retryreads is false in the URI the retry behavior does not apply. Call this method on the database which the change stream listens to. pipelineJSON: A JSON representing an aggregation pipeline appended to the change stream. This may be an empty document. OptionsJSON: A JSON containing change stream options. options may be "" or a JSON document with additional command options: batchSize: An integer representing number of documents requested to be returned on each call to NextChange() resumeAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with startAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAfter: A Document representing the logical starting point of the change stream. Unlike resumeAfter, this can resume notifications after an "invalidate" event. The result of StreamResumeToken() or the _id field of any change received from a change stream can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAtOperationTime. startAtOperationTime: A Timestamp. The change stream only provides changes that occurred at or after the specified timestamp. Any command run against the server will return an operation time that can be used here. This option is mutually exclusive with resumeAfter and startAfter. maxAwaitTimeMS: An int64 representing the maximum amount of time a call to StreamResumeToken() will block waiting for data fullDocument: An optional UTF-8 string. functions.name: Path.AddPathComponent
functions.description: Adds a new path component. The path provided as parameter does not need to end with a slash or backslash. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Component parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path.

parameters.name: Component...
parameters.description: The new path component.

examples.label: Import all files in a folder:
examples.text_code:
# list files in a folder
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Files.List
";
$FolderPath
) ]
# loop over the files
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount (
$r
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$count
>0 ]
Loop
# build path
Set Variable [
$fileName
; GetValue (
$r
;
$index
+1  )]
Set Variable [
$filePath
; Value:
MBS
( "Path.AddPathComponent";
$FolderPath
;
$fileName
)]
Set Variable [
$fileData
; Value:
MBS
("
Container.ReadFile
";
$filePath
)]
# create record for new file
New Record/Request
Set Field [
MyData::Container
;
$fileData
]
Set Field [
MyData::Name
;
$fileName
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
=
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> list files in a folder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Files.List"; $FolderPath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over the files</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount ( $r ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count>0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> build path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue ( $r ; $index+1  )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $FolderPath; $fileName )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$filePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Container.ReadFile"; $filePath)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$fileData</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> create record for new file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileData </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Container"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$fileName </Calculation>
    <Field table="MyData" id="99999" name="Name"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index = $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath
functions.description: Converts a FileMaker path useful for "filemac:" and "filewin:" path specification to a native path. The difference between a native path and a FileMaker path on Mac is, that FileMaker expects to have the Disk Name in front followed by the rest of the path. macOS functions require paths from the root disk to start without volume name. Other disks have paths starting with "/Volumes/name". This function does not work for relative paths. For version 11.0 we build in a check to pass through valid native paths. You should not need this feature as you care about your paths and you know what path you put into what variable, right? See also Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath for the reverse.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The FileMaker file path.

parameters.name: PassThroughNativePath
parameters.description: Whether to pass through a native path directly and avoid conversion, if the path is valid already. Default is 1 to enable this.

examples.label: Convert path to native path:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Path.FilemakerPathToNativePath"; "/Macintosh HD/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.FilemakerPathToNativePath"; "/Macintosh HD/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath";
"file:test/picture.jpg¶" &
"fileLinux:/test/picture.jpg¶" &
"fileMac:/Macintosh HD/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg¶" &
"fileWin:D:\test\picture.jpg" )
// pick the right path from a list of paths.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath"; 
"file:test/picture.jpg¶" & 
"fileLinux:/test/picture.jpg¶" & 
"fileMac:/Macintosh HD/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg¶" & 
"fileWin:D:\test\picture.jpg" )
// pick the right path from a list of paths.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Batch convert list of paths:
examples.text_code:
# test kopie20 in file Contacts
Set Variable [
$FMPaths
; Value: "/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture1.jpg" & ¶ &
"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture2.jpg" & ¶ &
"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture3.jpg" & ¶ &
"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture4.jpg" & ¶ &
"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture5.jpg" & ¶ &
"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture6.jpg"]
Set Variable [
$NativePaths
; Value: While (
[ list = ""; i = 1; c = ValueCount (
$FMPaths
) ] ;
i ≤ c ;
[ path = GetValue(
$FMPaths
; i); npath =
MBS
( "Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath"; path ); list = list & npath & ¶; i = i + 1 ] ; list ) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Native Paths" ;
$NativePaths
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> test kopie20 in file Contacts</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture1.jpg" & ¶ &	"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture2.jpg" & ¶ &	"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture3.jpg" & ¶ & 	"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture4.jpg" & ¶ & 	"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture5.jpg" & ¶ & 	"/Mac/Users/test/Desktop/picture6.jpg"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FMPaths</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While (	[ list = ""; i = 1; c = ValueCount ( $FMPaths ) ] ; 	i ≤ c ; 	[ path = GetValue($FMPaths; i); npath = MBS( "Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath"; path ); list = list & npath & ¶; i = i + 1 ] ; list ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$NativePaths</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Native Paths" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$NativePaths </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Text> write file with MBS</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; "Hello World"; $tempMBS; "UTF-8") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now have FileMaker email it with the FileMaker style path</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="63" name="Send Mail">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <DisableStepCollapsed state="False"/>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <MultipleEmails state="False"/>
    <SendViaSMTP state="False"/>
    <SendViaOAuthAuthentication state="False"/>
    <SMTPEncryptionType type="SMTPEncryptionNone"/>
    <SMTPAuthenticationType type="SMTPAuthenticationNone"/>
    <OAuthProvider type="OAuthProviderGoogle"/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Write a HTML file and an image file to load in web viewer:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [ $folder ; Value: MBS ( " Folders.UserTemporary " ) ] Set Variable [ $path ; Value: MBS ( " Path.AddPathComponent "; $folder ; "test.jpg" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " Container.WriteFile "; Images::Image ; $path ) ] # Set Variable [ $html ; Value: "

Hello World

" ] Set Variable [ $path ; Value: MBS ( " Path.AddPathComponent "; $folder ; "test.html" ) ] Set Variable [ $r ; Value: MBS ( " Text.WriteTextFile "; $html ; $path ) ] # Set Variable [ $URL ; Value: MBS ( "Path.FilePathToFileURL"; $Path ) ] Set Web Viewer [ Object Name: "web" ; URL: $URL ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserTemporary" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$folder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; "test.jpg" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.WriteFile"; Images::Image; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"<html><body><p>Hello World</p><img src=test.jpg width=300></body></html>" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$html</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $folder; "test.html" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $html; $path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.FilePathToFileURL"; $Path ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$URL</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="146" name="Set Web Viewer">
    <Action value="GoToURL"/>
    <ObjectName>
      <Calculation>"web"</Calculation>
    </ObjectName>
    <URL custom="False">
      <Calculation>$URL </Calculation>
    </URL>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: Path.NativePathToFileMakerPath
functions.description: Converts a native path to a FileMaker path useful for "filemac:" and "filewin:" path specification. The difference between a native path and a FileMaker path on Mac is, that FileMaker expects to have the Disk Name in front followed by the rest of the path. macOS functions require paths from the root disk to start without volume name. Other disks have paths starting with "/Volumes/name". This function does not work for relative paths.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path.

examples.label: Convert path to FileMaker path:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "Path.NativePathToFilemakerPath"; "/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg")
examples.xml_code:

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.NativePathToFilemakerPath"; "/Users/test/Desktop/picture.jpg")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build temp path:

functions.name: PhotoPicker.Available
functions.description: Queries whether the photo picker is available. Returns 1 on iOS 14.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.BytesLoaded
functions.description: Queries the number of bytes loaded.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.Clear
functions.description: Clears the picker. This will delete all temporary image files and frees memory

functions.name: PhotoPicker.Dismiss
functions.description: Dismisses the dialog.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.Error
functions.description: Queries error for loading an image. This error may tell you when a picture count not be loaded.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image. From 0 to PhotoPicker.ImageCount -1.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.File
functions.description: Queries native file path for the image.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image. From 0 to PhotoPicker.ImageCount -1.

examples.label: Process all the images:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$ImageCount
; Value:
MBS
("
PhotoPicker.ImageCount
") ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$index
<
$ImageCount
]
Loop
# Process each image
Set Variable [
$FilePath
; Value:
MBS
("PhotoPicker.File";
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$Error
; Value:
MBS
("
PhotoPicker.Error
";
$index
) ]
#
If [ Length (
$FilePath
) > 0 ]
New Record/Request
If [ Right (
$FilePath
; 5 ) = ".heic" ]
# special case for HEIC images -> we convert them to PNG
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadImageFile
";
$FilePath
; "PNG"; "image.png") ]
Else
# all other files get imported
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$FilePath
) ]
End If
Set Field [
ImagePicker::Picture
;
$image
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$ImageCount
]
End Loop
End If
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
PhotoPicker.Clear
") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PhotoPicker.ImageCount") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$ImageCount</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index < $ImageCount </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Process each image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PhotoPicker.File"; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FilePath</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PhotoPicker.Error"; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( $FilePath ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Right ( $FilePath; 5 ) = ".heic" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> special case for HEIC images -> we convert them to PNG</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadImageFile"; $FilePath; "PNG"; "image.png") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> all other files get imported</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Container.ReadFile"; $FilePath) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$image </Calculation>
    <Field table="ImagePicker" id="99999" name="Picture "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $ImageCount </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PhotoPicker.Clear") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Image</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PhotoPicker.GetDisabledCapabilities

functions.description: Queries the selection limit.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.ImageCount
functions.description: Queries number of images selected.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.IsLoading
functions.description: Queries whether images are still loading. Returns 1 while images load in background. You may want to make a loop and show progress if needed. Of course for one image, you may just show a layout with a loading message and wait. See also PhotoPicker.TotalBytes and PhotoPicker.BytesLoaded functions.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.IsPresented
functions.description: Checks whether panel is displayed. Returns 1 if visible and 0 if not.

functions.name: PhotoPicker.Present
functions.description: Presents the photo picker view. This will show the user interface and start the process. Your script may call this in a script and then wait for the script trigger. When the script is called, we close the picker automatically. macOS supported added for MBS Plugin v13.2. We open a popover window to pick, so you may want to trigger this from a button, so the popover is shown next to the mouse cursor on the button.

$r
; Value:

( "
PhotoPicker.Clear
") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:

( "
PhotoPicker.SetScript
"; Get(FileName); "GotImage") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
PhotoPicker.SetSelectionLimit
"; 10) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "PhotoPicker.Present") ]

functions.name: PortMidi.Abort
functions.description: Terminates outgoing messages immediately. The caller should immediately close the output port; this call may result in transmission of a partial midi message. There is no abort for Midi input because the user can simply ignore messages in the buffer and close an input device at any time.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

functions.name: PortMidi.CheckHostError

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

functions.result: Returns any pending host error text or nothing.

functions.name: PortMidi.Close

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close.

functions.name: PortMidi.OpenInput
functions.description: Opens an input device. Midi reference numbers are starting at 17000 and counting up for each new midi stream.

parameters.name: DeviceID
parameters.description: The device ID, a number from 0 to PortMidi.DeviceCount -1.

parameters.name: BufferSize
parameters.description: Optional, number of events to buffer. Default is 100 if you don't pass this parameter.

parameters.name: Latency
parameters.description: The desired latency. By default 0 is used which means no latency.

functions.name: PortMidi.OpenOutput
functions.description: Opens an output device. Midi reference numbers are starting at 17000 and counting up for each new midi stream.

parameters.name: DeviceID
parameters.description: The device ID, a number from 0 to PortMidi.DeviceCount -1.

parameters.name: BufferSize
parameters.description: Optional, Specifies the number of output events to be buffered waiting for output. Default is 100 if you don't pass this parameter.

parameters.name: Latency
parameters.description: The latency you want. Default is zero. Latency is the delay in milliseconds applied to timestamps to determine when the output should actually occur. (If latency is < 0, 0 is assumed.) If latency is zero, timestamps are ignored and all output is delivered immediately. If latency is greater than zero, output is delayed until the message timestamp plus the latency. In some cases, PortMidi can obtain better timing than your application by passing timestamps along to the device driver or hardware. Latency may also help you to synchronize midi data to audio data by matching midi latency to the audio buffer latency.

functions.name: PortMidi.Poll
functions.description: Tests whether input is available. Returns 1 if data is available and zero if not.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

functions.name: PortMidi.ReadMessage
functions.description: Reads one Midi message from the buffers. Format of result is a list containing: Status, Data1, Data2, Timestamp and raw message first as decimal number and then also as hex number. Hex notation makes reading easier for debugging. e.g. 144 is the status number for channel 1 note on followed by the Data1 as the note number and Data2 with the velocity. Status 128 is to turn the channel 1 note off.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

examples.label: Split MIDI Timestamp:
examples.text_code:
# last five bits are frame counter
Set Variable [
$Frame
;
MBS
("
Math.BitwiseAND
"; TimeStamp; 31)]
# Seconds are in 2nd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 8 to right
Set Variable [
$Second
;
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 8 ); 63) ]
# Minutes are in 3rd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 16 to right
Set Variable [
$Minute
;
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 16 ); 63) ]
# Hours are in 4th byte: take 5 bits after shifting 24 to right
Set Variable [
$Hour
;
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseShiftRight
"; TimeStamp; 24 ); 31)]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> last five bits are frame counter</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Math.BitwiseAND"; TimeStamp; 31)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Frame</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Seconds are in 2nd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 8 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 8 ); 63) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Second</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Minutes are in 3rd byte, so take 6 bits after shifting 16 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 16 ); 63) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Minute</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Hours are in 4th byte: take 5 bits after shifting 24 to right</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "Math.BitwiseShiftRight"; TimeStamp; 24 ); 31)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Hour</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PortMidi.SendMessage
functions.signature: MBS( "PortMidi.SendMessage"; StreamRef; Status; Data1; Data2 { ; TimeStamp } )
functions.description: Sends a midi message. The Status is the command code. 144 is note on and 128 is note off. For those note commands Data1 is the number of the note. 48 (4*12) is C2 and then you can count over all notes till you get to next octave with C3 (5*12).

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Status
parameters.description: The Midi status code.

parameters.name: Data1
parameters.description: The first data byte.

parameters.name: Data2
parameters.description: The second data bytes.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp. If zero or not provided, note is sent immediately.

examples.label: Press down a C note:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "PortMidi.SendMessage";
$StreamRef
; 144; 4*12+0; 127 )

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PortMidi.SendMessage"; $StreamRef; 144; 4*12+0; 127 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PortMidi.SendMessageRaw
functions.description: Sends a raw message. This allows you to encode your own message data like this: status + data1 * 256 + data2 * 65536 + data3 * 16777216. You can send this way also 4 byte messages by including a third data item.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: The message encoded as a 32bit integer number.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp.

functions.name: PortMidi.SendSysExHex
functions.description: Writes a timestamped system-exclusive midi message. The input text must be hex encoded and is decoded before sending.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: The message to send.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The timestamp.

examples.label: Send SysEx:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "PortMidi.SendSysExHex";
$out
; "F000200D681020002BF7" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PortMidi.SendSysExHex"; $out; "F000200D681020002BF7" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PortMidi.SetChannelMask
functions.description: Filters incoming messages based on channel. The mask is a 16-bit bitfield corresponding to appropriate channels. Pass sum of the channel mask values you need:

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Mask
parameters.description: The new mask.

functions.name: PortMidi.SetDataAvailableEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the data available evaluation for this midi port. When new data arrives, this expression is evaluated, so you can call PortMidi.ReadMessage function. Be aware that expression may be evaluated when just a few notes are in the read buffer. So the data message you wait for may only be available partly. In the expression the text $$ID$$ will be replaced when evaluated with the port midi reference, so you can pass it to other functions if needed.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: PortMidi.SetDataAvailableHandler
functions.description: Sets the data available handler for this midi port. When new data arrives, this script is called, so you can call PortMidi.ReadMessage function. Be aware that script may be called when just a few notes are in the read buffer.

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Set script to be triggered if data is available:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("PortMidi.SetDataAvailableHandler";
$$port
; Get(FileName); "ReadMessage")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("PortMidi.SetDataAvailableHandler"; $$port; Get(FileName); "ReadMessage")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PortMidi.SetFilter
functions.description: Sets filters on an open input stream to drop selected input types. By default, only active sensing messages are filtered. Filtering is useful when midi routing or midi thru functionality is being provided by the user application. For example, you may want to exclude timing messages (clock, MTC, start/stop/continue), while allowing note-related messages to pass. Or you may be using a sequencer or drum-machine for MIDI clock information but want to exclude any notes it may play. Filter values (can be ORed):

parameters.name: StreamRef
parameters.description: The reference to the midi stream. You create those with PortMidi.OpenInput or PortMidi.OpenOutput and finally close them with PortMidi.Close .

parameters.name: Filter
parameters.description: The new filter value.

examples.label: To prohibit, say, active sensing and sysex messages, call:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "PortMidi.SetFilter";
$StreamRef
; 16384 + 1 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "PortMidi.SetFilter"; $StreamRef; 16384 + 1 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: PortMidi.TimeStamp
functions.description: Queries current time stamp. On Mac this is the time since booting in milliseconds.

functions.name: SharedMemory.DeleteValue
functions.description: Deletes a shared memory value. On Windows the shared memory only gets deleted if the last handle to it is freed.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the shared memory object.

functions.name: SharedMemory.Exists
functions.description: Checks whether shared memory exists.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the shared memory object.

examples.label: Check if shared memory object named test exists:

( "SharedMemory.Exists"; "test" )
Example result:
0
examples.xml_code:

functions.name: SharedMemory.GetValue
functions.description: Queries shared memory value. MBS Plugin provides SharedMemory functions to store UTF-8 text in shared memory, so one process can set it and other can read it, e.g. two versions of FileMaker Pro or two server processes. This function returns an error if variable doesn't exist. SharedMemory.LookupValue can query value (or default value).

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the shared memory object.

examples.label: Query shared memory object:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SharedMemory.GetValue"; "test")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SharedMemory.GetValue"; "test")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: SharedMemory.LookupValue
functions.description: Queries shared memory value. MBS Plugin provides SharedMemory functions to store UTF-8 text in shared memory, so one process can set it and other can read it, e.g. two versions of FileMaker Pro or two server processes. This function does return default value instead of an error. But it may still return an error if some technical problem exists for reading shared memory.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the shared memory object.

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: The default value to return if shared memory doesn't exist.

functions.name: SharedMemory.SetValue
functions.description: Sets shared memory value. MBS Plugin provides SharedMemory functions to store UTF-8 text in shared memory, so one process can set it and other can read it, e.g. two versions of FileMaker Pro or two server processes. The value is lost when computer restarts, but stays when application quits. On Windows the shared memory objects can't be accessed from another login session. To create a truly global one, we would need to run FileMaker as administrator and prefix the name with "Global\". And Windows deletes the shared memory when the last application referencing it closes.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the shared memory object.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The text value to store. e.g. an UUID to make it unique.

examples.label: Store text in shared memory:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "SharedMemory.SetValue"; "test"; "Hello World" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "SharedMemory.SetValue"; "test"; "Hello World" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

MBS( "UserNotification.ClearDidActivateNotificationScript" )</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

  </Step>

functions.name: UserNotification.ContentImage
functions.description: Queries image shown in the content of the notification. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG, PNG, GIF or BMP. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

functions.name: UserNotification.Create
functions.description: Creates a new user notification. All UserNotification functions require macOS 10.8. This is for sending user notifications to the Mac your FileMaker solution runs on. Not for remote notifications or notifications to iOS devices.
functions.result: Returns new user notification reference number.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetActionButtonTitle
functions.description: Queries current action button title. See also UserNotification.SetActionButtonTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetActivationType
functions.description: Queries how the notification was activated. Returns values like "none", "ContentsClicked" or "ActionButtonClicked".

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetActualDeliveryDate
functions.description: Queries actual delivery date. The date at which this notification was actually delivered. The notification center will set this value if a notification is put in the scheduled list and the delivery time arrives. If the notification is delivered directly using the UserNotification.deliverNotification function, this value will be set to the deliveryDate value (unless deliveryDate is not defined, in which case this value is set to the current date). This value is used to sort the list of notifications in the user interface.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetDeliveryDate
functions.description: Queries the planned delivery date. See also UserNotification.SetDeliveryDate

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetDeliveryTimeZone
functions.description: Queries the time zone for this notification. See also UserNotification.SetDeliveryTimeZone

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetDescription
functions.description: Queries description for debugging. This is an information text to show content to developer, not to end user.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetIdentifier
functions.description: Queries the identifier used to uniquely identify a notification. A notification delivered with the same identifier as an existing notification will replace that notification, rather than display a new one. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetInformativeText
functions.description: Queries the body text of the notification. See also UserNotification.SetInformativeText

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetOtherButtonTitle
functions.description: Queries the other button's custom title. See also UserNotification.SetOtherButtonTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetResponse
functions.description: Queries the response text. When a notification has been responded to, the didActivateNotification script will be called with the notification with the activationType set to Replied and the response set on the response property Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetResponsePlaceholder
functions.description: Queries the optional placeholder for inline reply field. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetSoundName
functions.description: Queries sound name for notification. See also UserNotification.SetSoundName

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetSubTitle
functions.description: Queries the current sub title of the notification. See also UserNotification.SetSubTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetTitle
functions.description: Queries current title of notification. See also UserNotification.SetTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.GetUserInfo
functions.description: Queries application specific user info data. Here you can query the data you included in the notification using SetUserInfo. See also UserNotification.SetUserInfo

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key for the entry.

functions.name: UserNotification.HasReplyButton
functions.description: Queries whether the notification has a reply button. The default value is false. If both this and hasActionButton are true, the reply button will be shown. Requires macOS 10.9. Returns 1 if reply button exists or 0 if not.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.Release
functions.description: Releases a notification reference. It is recommended that you release references which you do no longer need to reduce memory usage.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Frees all user notification objects.

$r
; Value:

( "UserNotification.ReleaseAll" ) ]

functions.name: UserNotification.SetActionButtonTitle
functions.description: Sets the title of the button displayed in the notification. Must be localized as it will be presented to the user. String will be truncated to a length appropriate for display. See also UserNotification.GetActionButtonTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new button title.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetContentImage
functions.description: Sets image shown in the content of the notification. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: the new image as container value. Can be JPEG, PNG or other formats supported by macOS.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetDeliveryDate
functions.description: Sets the delivery date. Specifies when (in an absolute time) the notification should be delivered. After a notification is delivered, it may be presented to the user. See also UserNotification.GetDeliveryDate

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: TimeStamp
parameters.description: The time stamp for the date and time to use for delivery.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetDeliveryTimeZone
functions.description: Set the time zone to interpret the delivery date in. If this value is nil and the user switches time zones, the notification center will adjust the time of presentation to account for the time zone change. If a notification should be delivered at a time in a specific time zone (regardless if the user switches time zones), set this value to that time zone. One common value may be the current time zone. See also UserNotification.GetDeliveryTimeZone

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the time zone.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetDidActivateNotificationScript
functions.description: Sets the activation notification action handler. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a notification has been activated. This gives you the possibility to react to the notification activation. The script parameter will be the notification reference. See also UserNotification.ClearDidActivateNotificationScript You can use UserNotification.SetUserInfo to pass individual data like record IDs with notification and later query it with UserNotification.GetUserInfo function. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag action handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "UserNotification.SetDidActivateNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "NotificationActivatedScript")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UserNotification.SetDidActivateNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "NotificationActivatedScript")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: UserNotification.SetDidDeliverNotificationScript
functions.description: Sets the delivery notification action handler. Specify a filename and script name to have this script being called when a notification has been delivered. This gives you the possibility to react to the notification delivery. The script parameter will be the notification reference. See also UserNotification.ClearDidDeliverNotificationScript You can use UserNotification.SetUserInfo to pass individual data like record IDs with notification and later query it with UserNotification.GetUserInfo function. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

examples.label: Registers drag action handler:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "UserNotification.SetDidDeliverNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "NotificationDeliveredScript")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UserNotification.SetDidDeliverNotificationScript"; Get ( FileName ); "NotificationDeliveredScript")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: UserNotification.SetHasActionButton
functions.description: Sets whether notification has an action button. Set to 0 if the notification has no action button. This will be the case for notifications that are purely for informational purposes and have no user action. The default value is 1.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new action button state.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetHasReplyButton
functions.description: Sets whether the notification has a reply button. The default value is false. If both this and hasActionButton are true, the reply button will be shown. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be 0 to disable or 1 to enable.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetIdentifier
functions.description: Sets the identifier used to uniquely identify a notification. A notification delivered with the same identifier as an existing notification will replace that notification, rather than display a new one. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Identifier
parameters.description: The new identifier.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetInformativeText
functions.description: Sets the body of the notification. Must be localized as it will be presented to the user. String will be truncated to a length appropriate for display. See also UserNotification.GetInformativeText

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new notification body text.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetOtherButtonTitle
functions.description: Set the localized string to customize the title of the 'Close' button in an alert-style notification. An empty string will cause the default localized text to be used. See also UserNotification.GetOtherButtonTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Title
parameters.description: The title of the button.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetResponsePlaceholder
functions.description: Sets the optional placeholder for inline reply field. Requires macOS 10.9.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new placeholder text.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetSoundName
functions.description: Sets sound name. The name of the sound file in the resources of the application bundle to play when the notification is delivered. "DefaultSoundName" can be used to play the default Notification Center sound. A value of "" means no sound. See also UserNotification.GetSoundName

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: SoundName
parameters.description: The new sound name.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetSubTitle
functions.description: Sets the subtitle displayed in the notification. Must be localized as it will be presented to the user. String will be truncated to a length appropriate for display. See also UserNotification.GetSubTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new subtitle for the notification.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetTitle
functions.description: Sets the title of the notification. Must be localized as it will be presented to the user. String will be truncated to a length appropriate for display. See also UserNotification.GetTitle

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The new title for the notification.

functions.name: UserNotification.SetUserInfo
functions.description: Stores Application-specific user info that may be retrieved later. You can store several text values with different keys. The userInfo should be of reasonable serialized size (less than 1k) or an exception will be thrown. This way you can pass details to the activation/deliver scripts. See also UserNotification.GetUserInfo

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The key for the new entry.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value for the new entry.

functions.name: UserNotification.deliverNotification
functions.description: Deliver a notification immediately, including animation or sound alerts. It will be presented to the user (subject to user preferences). UserNotification.isPresented will always return 1 if a notification is delivered using this method. If notification is not showing, please check system preferences to see whether FileMaker is allowed to post notifications and what types are allowed. e.g. with action or not. Notification by default do not show while a keynote presentation is running as OS X delays them.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.deliveredNotifications
functions.description: Queries list of all delivered notifications. Get a list of notification references that have been delivered to the Notification Center. The number of notifications the user actually sees in the user interface may be less than the size of this array. Note that these may or may not have been actually presented to the user (see UserNotification.isPresented ).

functions.name: UserNotification.hasActionButton
functions.description: Queries whether notification has an action button.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.isPresented
functions.description: Whether the notification has been presented. In some cases, e.g. when your application is frontmost, the notification center may decide not to actually present a delivered notification. In that case, the value of this property will be 0. It will be set to 1 if the notification was presented according to user preferences (note: this can mean no dialog, animation, or sound, if the user has turned off notifications completely for your application).

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.isRemote
functions.description: Queries whether this is a remote notification. With FileMaker 11 or newer there is no way to receive push notifications as FileMaker is not in the app store.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.result: Returns 1 if the user notification is from a remote (push) notification.

functions.name: UserNotification.removeAllDeliveredNotifications
functions.description: Clear all delivered notifications for this application from the notification center.

functions.name: UserNotification.removeAllScheduledNotifications
functions.description: Remove all scheduled user notifications from the user notification center.

functions.name: UserNotification.removeDeliveredNotification
functions.description: Clear a delivered notification from the notification center. If the notification is not in the delivered list, nothing happens.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.removeScheduledNotification
functions.description: Cancels a notification. If the deliveryDate occurs before the cancellation finishes, the notification may still be delivered. If the notification is not in the scheduled list, nothing happens.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.scheduleNotification
functions.description: Add a notification to the center for scheduling. If notification is not showing, please check system preferences to see whether FileMaker is allowed to post notifications and what types are allowed. e.g. with action or not. Notification by default do not show while a keynote presentation is running as OS X delays them.

parameters.name: UserNotificationReference
parameters.description: An user notification reference.

functions.name: UserNotification.scheduledNotifications
functions.description: Queries list of all delivered notifications. Get a list of notifications that are scheduled but have not yet been presented. Newly scheduled notifications are added to the end of the array.

functions.name: WIA.ClearCurrentDevice
functions.description: Clears current device.

functions.name: WIA.DeviceCount
functions.description: Queries number of devices.

examples.label: Walk over list of devices and query properties:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [“WIA Scan” (WIA Scan)]
Delete All Records [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
("WIA.DeviceCount")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["Error";
$c
]
Else
If [
$c
> 0]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:0]
Loop
Perform Script [“List Properties”; Parameter:
$i
]
#Next
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+ 1]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>=
$c
]
End Loop
End If
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="6" name="Go to Layout">
    <LayoutDestination value="SelectedLayout"/>
    <Layout id="99999" name="WIA Scan"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="10" name="Delete All Records">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WIA.DeviceCount")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$c</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"Error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$c</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>$c > 0</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>$i</Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="List Properties"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text>Next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i + 1</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation>$i >= $c</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$list

$device

("IsError")]

$list
]

; Value:ValueCount (
$list
)]
If [
$count
> 0]

; Value:1]

Set Variable [
$key
; Value:GetValue (
$list
;
$i
)]

("
WIA.DevicePropertyValue
";
$device
;
$key
)]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Device
;
$device
]
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Key
;
$key
]
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Value
;
$value
]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
#Next

$i
; Value:
$i
+1]

$i
>
$count
]

End If
End If

functions.name: WIA.Image
functions.description: Queries native path to image file. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the image from 0 to WIA.ImageCount -1.

examples.label: Import Images Script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
WIA.ImageCount
") ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:0 ]
Go to Layout [ “Images” (Images) ]
Loop
Set Variable [
$ImagePath
; Value:
MBS
( "WIA.Image";
$i
) ]
If [
MBS
( "
Files.FileExists
";
$ImagePath
) ]
Set Variable [
$image
; Value:
MBS
( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$ImagePath
) ]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Images::Name
;
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$ImagePath
) ]
Set Field [
Images::Image
;
$image
]

functions.name: WIA.ImageDialog
functions.description: Offer dialog to user to acquire image. This may ask the user for the device to use. If a current device is selected, this one will be used. When the functions returns, you can use WIA.ImageCount to query how many images got scanned. Even if the user cancels, there may be some images already available. Windows error -2145320939 or hex 0x80210008 is documented by Microsoft as WIA_S_NO_DEVICE_AVAILABLE and means that you have no device, or just no driver to cover the device. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Specifies dialog box behavior. Currently only UseCommonUI is available. Use this flag to use the system UI instead of the vendor-supplied UI.

parameters.name: FolderPath
parameters.description: The native path to the folder where images are stored. Your application must have write permissions for this folder.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name ot use. Extensions and/or numbers are added if needed.

examples.label: Show image dialog:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$folder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserTemporary
" )]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:

( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$folder
; "FileMakerScans")]

; Value:

( "
Files.CreateDirectory
";
$path
)]
#Scan...

$r
; Value:

("WIA.ImageDialog"; "Default";
$path
; "scan")]
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
WIA.ImageCount
")]
#Result can be OK or Cancel and in both cases some images may be there
If [
$r
≠ "OK"]
Show Custom Dialog ["Scan Result";
$r
]
End If
If [
$count
> 0]
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:0]
Go to Layout [“Images” (Images)]
Loop
Set Variable [
$ImagePath
; Value:
MBS
( "
WIA.Image
";
$i
)]

; Value:

( "
Container.ReadFile
";
$ImagePath
)]
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Images::Name
;
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$ImagePath
)]
Set Field [
Images::Image
;
$image
]
Commit Records/Requests [No dialog]
#Next
Set Variable [
$i
; Value:
$i
+ 1]

$i
>=
$count
]

$item

;
$key

Set Field [

;
$value
]

# Next

$i
; Value:
$i
+1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$i
>
$count
]

End If
End If

    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue ( $list; $i) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("WIA.ItemPropertyValue"; $item; $key) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$value</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$item </Calculation>
    <Field table="WIA Scan" id="99999" name="Device "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$key </Calculation>
    <Field table="WIA Scan" id="99999" name="Key "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$value </Calculation>
    <Field table="WIA Scan" id="99999" name="Value "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$i+1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$i</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $i > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

$item
; Value: "Feeder" ]
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:

("
WIA.ItemPropertyKeys
";
$item
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error" ;
$list
]
Else

$count
; Value: ValueCount (
$list
) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$i

Loop
Set Variable [
$key

$list
;
$i

Set Variable [
$value

$item
;
$key

New Record/Request
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Device
;
$item
]
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Key
;
$key
]
Set Field [
WIA Scan::Value
;
$value
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
# Next

$i
; Value:
$i
+1 ]

$i
>
$count
]

functions.name: WIA.Scan
functions.description: Transfers data from the given item to the application. We normally put the files on successful transfer into temp file and you get paths back from WIA.Image function. If the document feeder fails to supply an image, the returned path to image file will be empty.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The item of current device to query. For a scanner this is typical Flatbed or Feeder. see WIA.ListItems
