examples.label: Scan duplex from document feeder:
examples.text_code:
# switch on duplex mode
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
WIA.SetItemPropertyValue
"; "Feeder"; "Document Handling Select"; 4 ) /* 1 = feeder 2 = flatbed 4 = duplex 8 = front first 16 = back first 32 = front only 64 = back only 128 = next page 256 = preferred 512 = auto advance see https://docs.microsoft.… ]
#
# Scan via document Feeder
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WIA.Scan"; "Feeder") ]
#
# Result can be OK or Cancel and in both cases some images may be there
If [
$r
≠ "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Scan Result" ;
$r
]
Else
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Import Images” ; Parameter:    ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> switch on duplex mode</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Feeder"; "Document Handling Select"; 4 ) /* 1 = feeder 2 = flatbed 4 = duplex 8 = front first 16 = back first 32 = front only 64 = back only 128 = next page 256 = preferred 512 = auto advance see https://docs.microsoft.… </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Scan via document Feeder</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WIA.Scan"; "Feeder") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Result can be OK or Cancel and in both cases some images may be there</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r ≠ "OK" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Scan Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Import Images"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WIA.SetDevicePropertyValue
functions.description: Sets a device property value. You can use WIA.DevicePropertyKeys to learn about available keys. To learn about properties you may need to visit Microsoft websites and figure out the options and the values for the listed constants: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/ms630196(v=vs.85).aspx Page sizes are listed here: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/aa360096(v=VS.85).aspx Please query with WIA.DeviceProperties to see what keys and values are possible. You may want to query before and after setting the value via dialog.

parameters.name: Device
parameters.description: You can pass index of device to check them in a row. Or pass name or unique ID of device. Pass empty string for current device.

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The property key.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The value to use.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type to use. By default if empty or auto, we detect it based on the type of the existing property. Can be Boolean, Int16, Int32, Int64, UInt16, UInt32, UInt64, String or Double. Added GUID and CLSID as type for v12.1.

functions.name: WIA.SetItemPropertyValue
functions.description: Sets a property of an item. Please query with WIA.ItemProperties to see what keys and values are possible. You may want to query before and after setting the value via dialog.

parameters.name: Item
parameters.description: The item of current device to query. For a scanner this is typical Flatbed or Feeder. see WIA.ListItems

parameters.name: Key
parameters.description: The property key. Can be a text with the name of the property or the corresponding ID.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new value for this property.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.description: The data type to use. By default if empty or auto, we detect it based on the type of the existing property. Can be Boolean, Int16, Int32, Int64, UInt16, UInt32, UInt64, GUID, CLSID, String or Double.

examples.label: Set resolution:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Flatbed"; "Horizontal Resolution"; 300 ) &
MBS
( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Flatbed"; "Vertical Resolution"; 300 )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Flatbed"; "Horizontal Resolution"; 300 ) & 
MBS( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Flatbed"; "Vertical Resolution"; 300 )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set duplex:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Feeder"; "Document Handling Select"; 4 )
/*
1 = feeder
2 = flatbed
4 = duplex
8 = front first
16 = back first
32 = front only
64 = back only
128 = next page
256 = preferred
512 = auto advance
see
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/image/wia-dps-document-handling-select
*/ ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Set duplex" ;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Feeder"; "Document Handling Select"; 4 )/*1 = feeder2 = flatbed4 = duplex8 = front first16 = back first32 = front only64 = back only128 = next page256 = preferred512 = auto advanceseehttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/image/wia-dps-document-handling-select*/ </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Set duplex" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set to ignore blank pages:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Feeder"; "Blank Pages"; 1 )
/*
0 = Blank page detection is disabled. This is the required default value if the property is supported.
1 = The device detects blank pages and automatically skips scanning them (discards scanned data if any) and continues scanning.
2 = The device detects blank pages and acts as configured through the Job Separators property. This value is valid only when the Feeder item supports the Job Separators property.
see
https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/image/wia-ips-blank-pages
*/ ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Set blank pages" ;
$r
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "WIA.SetItemPropertyValue"; "Feeder"; "Blank Pages"; 1 )/*0 = Blank page detection is disabled. This is the required default value if the property is supported.1 = The device detects blank pages and automatically skips scanning them (discards scanned data if any) and continues scanning.2 = The device detects blank pages and acts as configured through the Job Separators property. This value is valid only when the Feeder item supports the Job Separators property.seehttps://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-hardware/drivers/image/wia-ips-blank-pages*/ </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Set blank pages" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
